# Handoff Archive вЂ” TRACE Platform

### 2026-07-21 вЂ” THUNDER D-50 BUILD 2B: the RECONCILE SCREEN вЂ” a physical count becomes STAMPED, DATED, ATTRIBUTED truth on the append-only ledger. **The spec's two central formulas were both wrong; both were corrected and the corrections are mutation-proven.** BUILDER-COMPLETE; owner-prove OWED (9 cards). Zero api-fn (12/12), zero schema, zero migration.

**Type:** App code вЂ” **2 NEW** (`packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/InventoryReconcile.tsx` В· `src/lib/reconcileMath.ts` вЂ” the PURE decision) + **1 NEW test** (`src/lib/reconcileMath.test.ts`, **48/0**) + **3 EDIT** (`src/router.tsx` В· `src/registry/tileRegistry.ts` В· `src/pages/BusinessInventory.tsx`). Docs: ledger **#145** В· `built-inventory.md` (line 4 + NEW capability entry) В· owner-test board (**NEW `## SURFACE: reconcile`, 9 cards all `owed`**) В· `tech-debt-log.md` **#67 + #68 + #69 NEW** В· `DECISIONS-INDEX.md` drift line В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ В· this В§3 + line 3. **В§3 RETENTION: 1 archived verbatim (OWNER-GATED DEV SURFACES), 1 written вЂ” entries-in == entries-out, В§3 back to 3.** **NO new decision** (implements D-50; **R-STATUS and blind-capture deliberately left OPEN**). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII-export/webhooks/AI-calls/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** ("Reconcile the count against the ledger вЂ” leakage becomes arithmetic", ARC asset-inventory-pmi, MAPS-TO 2.3/5.1 вЂ” cited; its BUILD INPUT (1) *"where the real actor comes from"* is ANSWERED here (the signed-in desk user), BUILD INPUT (2) *blind capture* is NOT and stays David's), **3 VALIDATION** (negative/fractional counts and zero-qty attributions refused **before** the RPC; the over-attribution refusal NAMES the number it refused against), **4 CRUD-PERMISSIONS** (VIEW_COSTS route gate + `assert_movement_actor` server-side вЂ” defence in depth, not one lock), **6 AC-1/3** (no vertical noun; tenant scope untouched), **7 FUNCTION THRIFT** (zero api-fn вЂ” RPCs are postgres functions, reads run under existing RLS), **8 REUSE** (reserved/available through the ONE D-52 derivation, imported never re-exported), **9 TRACE STAYS ON** (`[TRACE:RECONCILE]` ON by birth), **11 OWNER-TEST** (9 cards, `owed`).

**вљ пёЏ THE HEADLINE: BOTH OF THE SPEC'S FORMULAS WOULD HAVE WRITTEN FALSE ROWS INTO A LOG THAT CANNOT BE RETRACTED.** **(A) `expected` is a FULL REPLAY, not `prior_count в€’ sales`.** The ledger records every movement вЂ” receive В· dead В· loss В· found В· adjust В· tombstone вЂ” not only sales. Subtracting sales alone makes any non-sale movement in the window surface as a residual: a **+10 receive becomes a в€’10 gap the owner is asked to explain as shrinkage that never happened.** That is D-9 *inverted* вЂ” it mis-attributes a REAL movement rather than fabricating one вЂ” and it asks a human to account, permanently and by name, for the system's own arithmetic error. **(B) Attribution SPLITS the delta; it does not add a second one.** `count_reconcile_inventory` and `adjust_inventory_manual` **both move qty**. Run in the specified sequence вЂ” count_reconcile for the whole change, *then* a dead/loss adjust per cause вЂ” they decrement **twice**: book 30, counted 13, attributed 4 dead + 3 lost lands on-hand at **6**, net **в€’24 instead of в€’17**. *Seven invented dead plants, unretractable.* Each cause now writes its own qty-moving event and the **unexplained remainder** lands on the closing `count_reconcile`, so the steps sum to exactly `counted в€’ book` and every cause gets the permanent dated line D-50's story literally asks for ("4 dead, 3 unexplained loss"). **Neither correction is a judgement call вЂ” each is proven by MUTATION TEST: reverting the code to the spec's version fails the suite (2 tests for A, 6 for B).**

**THE DECISION IS PURE, AND THAT IS THE ARCHITECTURE, NOT A PREFERENCE.** `reconcileMath.ts` performs no IO вЂ” mode detection, replay, residual, variance, and the ordered write PLAN are all functions of their inputs, and the page only executes what they return. Extracted pure for the same reason `countPromote.ts` was at D-49: **arithmetic that decides what enters an append-only log must be testable without a database, because a wrong row can only be apologized for with a second row.** Order inside the plan is load-bearing twice: **credits before debits** (a в€’6 dead before a +4 found on a lot of 3 would RAISE at the RPC and **strand half an attribution permanently**, with no way to finish it), and the **closing step lands ABSOLUTELY on `counted`** so a sale landing mid-plan is absorbed into that one row rather than silently lost. `miscount` is deliberately NOT an attribution kind вЂ” "the book was wrong, nothing physically happened" is *exactly* what a `count_reconcile` row already means, so it is the REMAINDER; giving it its own event would assert a movement that did not occur.

**BASELINE HIDES THE COLUMN RATHER THAN EMPTYING IT.** On a fresh tenant every lot is BASELINE and the "Since last count" column **does not exist** вЂ” not rendered blank. An empty sold-column on the screen built to prove how easy this is invites "why is this blank?", which is the opposite of the demo's point. It appears automatically the moment any lot has a window. Same reasoning made baseline ask for **no attribution**: there is no history to explain a first count against, so demanding one would be theatre.

**THE QUALITY GATE CAUGHT A REAL GAP I HAD NOT CONSIDERED.** `verify-universals` cap **#r** failed the first run: *"routes reachable but with NO nav entry (URL-only/orphaned): /inventory/reconcile."* The cheap fix was adding it to the EXCEPTIONS list beside `/inventory/count`. That would have been wrong вЂ” count is a sub-flow entered mid-task, but **reconcile is a destination the owner goes to deliberately**. It got a real `NAV_IA` node under Inventory, plus a "Reconcile count" button beside "Start count" on the grid. *"A new surface cannot ship without nav"* did its job.

**PROOFS.** `npm run verify` **exit 0, zero net-new** (tsc 5/5 В· eslint 248 В· knip 10/14/15) вЂ” and it went red first, correctly, three times: knip flagged 4 unused exports + 3 unused types in `reconcileMath.ts` (all genuinely module-internal вЂ” made private, **not re-baselined**, per the D-52 `holdsCommitment` precedent), eslint caught a floating promise and an async `onClick`, and `#r` caught the orphan route. `build:cultivar` exit 0. Tests **48/0**. Self-caught while writing the Accept sheet: Reserved was derived as `book в€’ available`, which **understates it whenever committed exceeds on-hand** (`availableFrom` floors at 0) вЂ” reserved is a read fact, not a subtraction, so it is now passed in.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** **вљ пёЏ #67 вЂ” BLIND CAPTURE IS THE MISSING HALF AND IT IS YOUR OPEN BUILD INPUT.** `InventoryCount.tsx:438` already calls `count_reconcile_inventory` **at capture**, so a phone count applies itself and a desk reconcile *of that walk* reads residual **0 by construction**. DELTA mode is real and provable for movements landing **after** a count (which is what owner-prove step 2 exercises), but it is **not** the count-then-review loop the story describes. `blind_capture_mode` is named in that story's own `PIECES:` вЂ” **does it ship, and is it default-blind?** **(b)** the 9 cards; sharpest are the **RECEIVE** card (a non-sale movement must read residual **0**, never phantom shrinkage) and the **ATTRIBUTION-SPLIT** card (read the "What Accept writes" box *before* confirming: 3 steps, ending on **13**, `SUM(delta) = в€’17` not в€’24). Then re-run LAYER 1's **V3(b)** вЂ” `qty == SUM(delta)`, 0 rows, with the new reconcile events in the sum. **(c)** **#68 NEW** вЂ” session scoping is **not built**, and the spec asked for it: blocked by #67, and `inventory_count_sessions.status` already has one owner (a second writer is the STD-011 drift we keep paying for). **(d)** **#69 NEW** вЂ” a multi-step accept can **partially land** (the sequence is not atomic even though each RPC is); mitigated by an honest stop-message naming the step and by the absolute closing step, durable fix is a one-RPC plan = a migration. **(e)** the `was_unknown` queue **READS but offers no Resolve button** вЂ” deliberate: a control that looks actionable and isn't is a dead affordance, and the card says so. **(f)** #56/#59/#60/#61/#62/#63/#64/#65/#66 unchanged.

<!-- Archived 2026-07-22 by the SELLABLE-PREDICATE close-out (В§3 RETENTION N=3, OP-13) вЂ” verbatim, not summarized. -->

### 2026-07-21 вЂ” THUNDER D-52: the THREE-NUMBER inventory model lands in CODE вЂ” on-hand stops moving at checkout and starts moving at FULFILLMENT. **The spec named 2 seams; there were 4, and the other 2 would have INVENTED stock.** BUILDER-COMPLETE; owner-prove OWED (8 cards); remediation REPORT-ONLY pending David's confirm. Zero api-fn (12/12), zero schema, zero migration.

**Type:** App code вЂ” **1 NEW** (`packages/cultivar-os/src/lib/inventoryStates.ts` вЂ” THE derivation home) + **1 NEW script** (`scripts/d52-remediate-committed-stock.mjs`) + **4 EDIT** (`api/orders/submit.ts` В· `api/dashboard.ts` В· `src/pages/BusinessInventory.tsx` В· `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`). Docs: ledger **#144** В· `built-inventory.md` (line 4 + the D-42 entry marked SUPERSEDED-IN-PART) В· owner-test board (**NEW `## SURFACE: inventory-states`, 8 cards all `owed`** + the D-50 checkout-sale card **amended** вЂ” it now says "run this as a WALK-IN only") В· `tech-debt-log.md` **#65 + #66 NEW** В· `DECISIONS-INDEX.md` (D-52 row flipped from DOCS-ONLY to BUILT + drift line) В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ В· this В§3 + line 3. **В§3 RETENTION: 1 archived verbatim (SHA STAMP), 1 written вЂ” entries-in == entries-out, В§3 back to 3.** **NO new decision** (implements D-52; **R-STATUS deliberately left OPEN**). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII-export/webhooks/AI-calls/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (D-52's, cited), **3 VALIDATION** (the refusal names both numbers, never a bare "0 in stock" against a lot the owner can see holding 12), **4 CRUD-PERMISSIONS** (unchanged вЂ” `handleStatus` remains manager-gated), **6 AC-1/3** (no vertical noun; tenant scope untouched), **7 FUNCTION THRIFT** (zero api-fn), **8 REUSE** (ONE derivation read by the checkout guard AND the grid AND the dashboard), **9 TRACE STAYS ON** (`[TRACE:INVENTORY]` ON by birth on every new branch), **11 OWNER-TEST** (8 cards, `owed`).

**вљ пёЏ THE HEADLINE: THE SPEC NAMED TWO SEAMS. THERE WERE FOUR вЂ” AND THE UNNAMED TWO WOULD HAVE MANUFACTURED PLANTS.** BUILD 1 correctly identified the checkout decrement and the missing `fulfilled` branch. But **four** call sites touch on-hand on the order path, and the other three вЂ” the **cancel-restore**, the **delete-restore**, and `handleUpdate`'s **edit-delta arithmetic** вЂ” were every one of them written believing *checkout had already decremented*. That belief was true under D-42 and became false the instant the decrement moved. Left alone, **cancelling a pending order would have credited stock back for a decrement that never happened** вЂ” inventing plants the nursery does not have, and D-50's append-only ledger would have recorded the invention as unretractable fact. The same for deleting an open order, and the edit path would have drifted on-hand by (old в€’ new) on orders that never took anything. **All four now key off ONE predicate вЂ” `movesOnHand(status)` вЂ” so there is a single answer to "did this order's stock physically move?" instead of four sites each assuming it.** That consolidation *is* the fix; moving the decrement was the easy half.

**вљ пёЏ THE REMEDIATION EXCLUDES WALK-INS, AND THE SPEC DID NOT SAY TO.** BUILD 5 said: for each unfulfilled order line, credit +qty back. Applied literally that **over-credits every self-transport order still sitting `pending`** вЂ” those customers paid and drove away with the plants the same day. The stock is genuinely gone; D-42 decremented it **correctly**; the status reads `pending` only because no transition UI ever existed to close it. Crediting them would invent stock in a log that cannot be retracted вЂ” the exact failure D-50's immutability exists to prevent, committed by our own hand. **The script therefore splits by `transport_method` and reports all three buckets (remediate В· skipped-walk-in В· skipped-no-lot) so David checks the judgement rather than trusting it.** It is **REPORT-ONLY by default**; `--apply` is opt-in and writes LEDGERED events (`kind='d42_remediation'`, `p_reason` naming the order), never a silent UPDATE. **I could not run the report myself вЂ” the sandbox blocks reading `.env` files, which is correct for secrets вЂ” so the 16-order list is David's to produce and confirm.** That constraint pushed the deliverable into the shape it should have had anyway: a gated script, like the migration.

**COMMITTED IS DERIVED, AND THAT WAS ONE OF D-52'S TWO OPEN BUILD INPUTS.** No column was added. `committed` = SUM of open order-line quantities; `available` = on-hand в€’ committed. A stored `committed` would be a **second representation of the open orders** and would drift from them (STD-011) вЂ” the orders ARE the commitment, so we read them. `holdsCommitment` is derived **by exclusion** (not `fulfilled`, not `cancelled`) rather than an allow-list, deliberately: **R-STATUS is still open**, and a new *open* state is likelier than a new terminal one вЂ” so an unratified future status defaults to **holding** its commitment (stock protected) instead of silently releasing it (stock oversold). **Fail toward not overselling.** Same reasoning made the walk-in's status load-bearing: it is born `fulfilled`, because leaving it `pending` would count units as committed that are already on a customer's trailer.

**THE CARD THAT JUSTIFIES THE BUILD.** Two delivery orders for the same units: the second is now **refused against AVAILABLE while the grid still shows the stock on hand**. Under D-42 that second order **succeeded** вЂ” selling the same physical plants twice. The refusal message names both numbers on purpose (*"Only 0 available to sell (12 on hand, 12 committed to open orders)"*), because a bare *"only 0 in stock"* against a lot the owner can see holding twelve reads as a bug, not a rule.

**PROOFS.** `npm run verify` **exit 0, zero net-new** (tsc 5/5 В· eslint 248 В· knip 10/14/15) вЂ” and it went red first: knip flagged `holdsCommitment` as an unused export, which was **correct** (nothing outside the module consumes it), so it was made internal rather than re-baselined. `build:cultivar` exit 0. **вљ пёЏ The gate is BLIND to the two `api/` files this build edits** (`tsconfig.json` в†’ `include: ["src"]`), so `submit.ts` + `dashboard.ts` were typechecked **directly** under `--strict` в†’ **exit 0**; *that* is the type evidence, not the green gate. Now recorded as **tech-debt #65** rather than re-flagged each build.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** run the **remediation REPORT** and confirm the two lists вЂ” especially that every "SKIPPED: walk-in" row is genuinely a customer who drove away. Nothing writes until you say so. **(b)** the 8 cards; sharpest are the **oversell-against-available** card and **"Cancelling an OPEN order invents no stock."** Then re-run Layer 1's **V3(b)** вЂ” `qty == SUM(delta)` must still be 0 rows *with the remediation events in the sum*. **(c)** **tech-debt #66 NEW** вЂ” the anonymous QR plant page still shows ON-HAND under the word "available", and **structurally cannot** show otherwise: `order_items` RLS is `authenticated_*` by policy (verified by reading it), so an anon customer cannot derive committed. The server refuses correctly, so it is a UX gap, not an oversell hole вЂ” **your call between a server-side derived read (mind В§6 r11, 12/12) and simply softening the copy to "in stock"**, which is nearly free. **(d)** **tech-debt #65 NEW** вЂ” the quality gate cannot see `api/`. **(e)** **R-STATUS is still OPEN** вЂ” this build deliberately did not ratify it, and #64 (`qbo/.../cultivar.ts` writing an undeclared `invoiced`) still means an order can reach a state that emits no lifecycle event. **(f)** #56/#59/#60/#61/#62/#63 unchanged.

### 2026-07-20 вЂ” THUNDER D-50 LAYER 2A-2: the ORDER PATH now writes to the ledger вЂ” lifecycle transitions AND the sale, one append-only event stream. **The spec's central premise was FALSE and was corrected, not built to.** BUILDER-COMPLETE; migration GATED; owner-prove OWED (6 cards). Zero api-fn (12/12).

**Type:** App code + schema вЂ” **1 NEW migration** (`supabase/migrations/20260720_ledger_event_store_columns.sql` вЂ” GATED, David applies) + **1 EDIT** (`packages/cultivar-os/api/orders/submit.ts`). Docs: ledger #143 В· `built-inventory.md` line 4 В· owner-test board (**NEW `## SURFACE: order-events`, 6 cards, all `owed`**) В· `tech-debt-log.md` **#64 NEW** В· `DECISIONS-INDEX.md` drift line В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ В· this В§3 + line 3. **В§3 RETENTION: 1 archived verbatim (D-50 Layer 1), 1 written вЂ” entries-in == entries-out, В§3 back to 3.** **NO new decision** (implements D-50; deliberately does NOT ratify R-STATUS). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII-export/webhooks/AI-calls/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (D-50's, cited; the CONFLICT clause fired вЂ” see below), **6 AC-1/2/3** (no vertical noun; tenant scope unchanged; the view inherits base-table RLS and widens nothing), **7 FUNCTION THRIFT** (zero api-fn вЂ” RPCs are postgres functions), **8 REUSE** (one order-event seam; the aggregate defaults live in the ONE emit function so all six LAYER 1 RPCs inherit them **unedited**), **9 TRACE STAYS ON** (`[TRACE:INVENTORY]`/`[TRACE:ROSTER]` ON by birth), **11 OWNER-TEST** (6 cards, `owed`).

**вљ пёЏ THE SPEC WAS BUILT ON A STATUS THAT DOES NOT EXIST вЂ” AND THAT IS THE HEADLINE.** BUILD 3 specified: *"the PAID transition ALSO writes the stock SALE event."* There is **no `paid` status**. `ORDER_STATUSES` is `pending | confirmed | fulfilled | cancelled` (`orderStatus.ts:13`) вЂ” the spec named THREE statuses that do not exist (`created`, `paid`, `delivered`). Worse: **no status transition decrements stock at all.** The decrement lives in `handleCreate` В§11, at **CHECKOUT**, where the order is born `pending`. So the specified build was unbuildable and its owner-prove step (*"David marks an order PAID through the app"*) was unperformable. **Where the false premise came from is the lesson:** the decrement block's own comment called itself *"the ORDER-PAID/CONFIRMED commitment point вЂ¦ the stock is committed here, at payment."* **A comment describing a payment lifecycle the code does not have** вЂ” read in good faith, it sent a build spec hunting a transition that was never there. **That comment is now corrected in place** (tech-debt **#61**'s exact class: a comment contradicting its own repo costs real reasoning). Per the **В§9 story-reconciliation gate** вЂ” *CONFLICT в†’ STOP, surface, resolve before building* вЂ” this went to David rather than being resolved by inventing statuses, which would have silently ratified **R-STATUS**, an OPEN decision `orderStatus.ts:7-8` explicitly reserves for him. **David ruled: build to the real four, date the sale at checkout, leave R-STATUS open.**

**вљ пёЏ THE BACKFILL WAS REFUSED, AND REFUSING IT IS THE BUILD'S BEST DECISION.** The spec asked for one `UPDATE` setting `event_type = kind` on the 131 existing rows вЂ” *"additive."* It is not additive; it is an amendment of history. `business_inventory_ledger` carries a trigger that rejects UPDATE/DELETE **even for `postgres`** вЂ” owner-proven V2 on 2026-07-20, and called out AT THE TIME as the whole point, *because it defends against our own future code*. The backfill would have required `ALTER TABLE вЂ¦ DISABLE TRIGGER`: **the ledger's first extension would have opened the exact door the ledger exists to weld shut.** And it was never needed вЂ” `event_type` IS `kind`, `aggregate_type` IS `'INVENTORY'`, `aggregate_id` IS `inventory_id` for every historical row, so storing them is a **second copy of one fact (STD-011)**. A read-view (`business_inventory_ledger_events`) resolves them with zero mutation, and **card V2 now PROVES no historical row was rewritten** (`untouched == total`). The guarantee survived its own first upgrade вЂ” which is the thing worth testing.

**WHAT SHIPPED.** Three nullable columns + an aggregate index + the read-view. `emit_inventory_movement` widened 9в†’12 args (**DROP-then-CREATE, not overload** вЂ” the LAYER 1 В§7a trap: a second overload makes every existing 9-arg call AMBIGUOUS and they all start failing); the new params DEFAULT to `'INVENTORY'` + `inventory_id`, **so all six LAYER 1 RPCs populate the aggregate columns correctly with zero edits to any of them.** New `record_order_event` вЂ” **`delta` is hard-coded 0 inside the RPC, not a parameter**, because an order event must never be able to move on-hand and making that un-passable beats trusting every future caller. Granted **service_role only**, deliberately NOT `authenticated`: `assert_movement_actor` stops a client forging someone else's id, but a signed-in manager with EXECUTE could still write arbitrary lifecycle events into a log nobody can retract. In `submit.ts`, all **4** `adjustLotQty` sites now carry actor + `kind` + `occurred_at` + `source_id=order`, and **4** order events emit (`order_created` В· `order_edited` В· `order_deleted` В· `order_${status}`). The transition event is **derived** (`order_${status}`), not a hand-maintained switch вЂ” when David ratifies R-STATUS the new statuses emit for free; a switch would silently miss them.

**THE NULL ACTOR IS A FEATURE AND THE CARD SAYS SO.** The spec said *"thread the real caller, do not pass null."* Half right. `handleCreate` is **anon-createable** вЂ” the customer QR checkout has no caller вЂ” and `assert_movement_actor` explicitly admits a NULL actor as an honest system write, with D-50 В§11 stating it must **never** be defaulted to the owner. So the actor is threaded wherever one exists (edit/delete/cancel/status, all manager-gated) and stays **NULL on anonymous checkout**. The owner-test card asserts that NULL is a **PASS** and that recording the owner's uid there is the **FAIL** вЂ” a fabricated actor is worse than an absent one (D-9).

**вљ пёЏ `npm run verify` IS BLIND TO THE FILE THIS BUILD EDITS.** The gate passed **zero net-new** (tsc 5/5 В· eslint 248 В· knip 10/14/15) вЂ” but `packages/cultivar-os/tsconfig.json` has `include: ["src"]`, so **`api/` is never typechecked** and the green gate says nothing about `submit.ts`. Confirmed by `tsc --listFiles | grep -c submit` в†’ **0**. `submit.ts` was therefore typechecked **directly** under `--strict` (exit 0), and *that* is the evidence вЂ” not the gate. `build:cultivar` exit 0. **Flagged as a real hole:** the deployed backend is exactly the surface the quality gate cannot see.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** **apply the migration** вЂ” every card fails honestly until then. **(b)** the 6 cards; sharpest are **V2** (`untouched == total`) and the **anonymous-NULL-actor PASS** card. Then re-run Layer 1's **V3(b)** вЂ” `qty == SUM(delta)` must still be 0 rows under the new writers. **(c)** tech-debt **#64 NEW** вЂ” `qbo/invoice/cultivar.ts:658` writes `status: 'invoiced'`, a value `ORDER_STATUSES` does not contain and `handleStatus` would reject; **flag-only per your ruling**, but note an order can now reach a state that emits **no** lifecycle event. **(d)** **Layer 2 for the COUNT path is still owed** вЂ” this build did the ORDER half; the count/desk-grid callers are a separate rewire. **(e)** the cancel-restore still adjusts stock *before* the status write (pre-existing, not introduced here) вЂ” if the status write throws, stock is restored and the order is not cancelled. The **ledger stays truthful either way** (it records the movement that physically happened, and the `order_cancelled` event is correctly not written), so this is a transactionality gap, not a ledger gap. **(f)** #56/#59/#60/#61/#62/#63 unchanged.


<!-- Archived 2026-07-21 by the D-50 BUILD 2B (reconcile screen) close-out (В§3 RETENTION N=3, OP-13) вЂ” verbatim, not summarized. -->

### 2026-07-20 вЂ” THUNDER OWNER-GATED DEV SURFACES + the stamp made ALWAYS-VISIBLE: the typed flags are gone, and **the panels no longer mount before login** вЂ” a leak closed STRUCTURALLY, not with a conditional. BUILDER-COMPLETE; owner-prove OWED (5 cards); **tech-debt #60 still NOT closed.** Zero api-fn (12/12), no schema, no migration.

**Type:** App code вЂ” **3 NEW** (`packages/shared/src/devtools/devSurfaces.ts` + `index.ts` вЂ” THE one gate В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/components/VersionStamp.tsx`) + **7 EDIT** (`App.tsx` В· `components/layout/AppLayout.tsx` В· `DebugPanel.tsx` В· `RhythmLogger.tsx` В· `shared/src/components/AppHeader.tsx` В· `vite.config.ts` В· `vite-env.d.ts`). Docs: ledger #142 В· `built-inventory.md` (2 NEW entries + 1 rewritten + line 4) В· owner-test board (**GATE 0 в‘  rewritten + 4 NEW cards**) В· `tech-debt-log.md` #60 В· `DECISIONS-INDEX.md` (drift + line 7) В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ В· this В§3 + line 3. Also commits the **2026-07-20 typed-activation recon** this build implements. **В§3 RETENTION: 1 archived verbatim (2026-07-17 OP-14), 1 written вЂ” entries-in == entries-out, В§3 back to 3.** **NO new decision.** **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII-export/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (implements the recon + OP-15, no new user story), **4 CRUD-PERMISSIONS** (owner-only, enforced by mount location AND by the toggle, not by hiding a control), **5 UI** (48px targets, visible On/Off state, no dead affordance), **6 AC-1/3/4** (vertical-neutral; identity-scoped; injected prop), **7 FUNCTION THRIFT** (zero api-fn), **8 REUSE** (this build IS В§6 r8), **9 TRACE STAYS ON** (`[TRACE:DEVGATE]` new, ON by birth), **11 OWNER-TEST** (5 cards, all `owed`).

**THE LEAK, AND WHY THE FIX IS STRUCTURAL.** Yesterday's recon found `DebugPanel` and `RhythmLogger` mounted in `App.tsx` as siblings of `<AppRouter/>` вЂ” **outside every auth gate**. So `?debug=1` worked **pre-login, on `/plant/:tagId`, the page a customer opens by scanning a tag in the lot** вЂ” and that panel prints tenant ids and emails by its own footer's admission. The fix was not to add an `isOwner &&`. The panels **moved into `AppLayout`**, which renders only inside `<PrivateRoute>`: **there is now no code path that mounts them for a signed-out visitor.** A conditional can be re-bypassed by the next person who edits it; a mount location cannot be bypassed without deleting the router. The owner-only toggle is then a *second* gate on top. **Neither is load-bearing alone вЂ” that is the point.**

**ONE GATE, NOT TWO (В§6 r8, the rule this build is an instance of).** `?debug=1` and `?rhythm=1` were the same OPERATION written twice: two `xEnabled()` readers, two sticky localStorage keys, two floating buttons deliberately placed on opposite corners so they wouldn't collide. Now: `packages/shared/src/devtools` вЂ” `useDevSurface` / `toggleDevSurface` / `bindDevSurfaceIdentity` / `clearDevSurfaces`. **The typed flags and the raw `traceDebug`/`traceRhythm` keys are DELETED** (grep-confirmed: the only remaining mentions are comments saying *don't bring these back*). State is keyed to **(userEmail + role)** and purged when either changes вЂ” the old keys were raw localStorage with **no session coupling**, so they survived logout *and* role change, and a device left in debug stayed in debug for whoever signed in next.

**вљ пёЏ THE JUDGEMENT CALL вЂ” AND IT CUTS AGAINST EVERYTHING ELSE IN THIS BUILD.** The version stamp is deliberately **NOT** behind the new owner gate, though every other dev surface moved behind it. #141 had put the SHA in the DebugPanel footer вЂ” which was correct *then*, and became **wrong the moment this build owner-gated that panel**: a deploy broken enough to break the menu would have **hidden its own tell**, which is precisely the failure tech-debt #60 exists to catch, reintroduced by the build hardening everything around it. So the stamp moved OUT to an always-visible `built <date> В· <sha>` line вЂ” every screen, every user, outside every gate, `pointer-events:none` so it can never swallow a tap вЂ” and was **removed from the panel** (STD-011: one home; two surfaces printing one fact drift, and a drifted version stamp asserts a build you are not running). **A tell must not depend on the thing it is testing.**

**TWO THINGS THE PROMPT ASSUMED THAT WERE NOT TRUE вЂ” checked, not inherited.** (1) It said the build "builds on the SHA/**BUILD** defines already live"; only `__COMMIT_SHA__` existed вЂ” **`__BUILD_TIME__` did not, and was added here.** (2) I nearly shipped a real bug: the context documents `role` as *"null for owners"* (BusinessProvider.tsx:137) and my gate treats a null role as signed-out вЂ” which would have made the gate **fail for owners, the only users it serves.** Reading further showed line 137 belongs to a *different* (membership) interface; the **context** `role` at line 165 is display-ready `'OWNER' | 'MANAGER' | 'STAFF'`. **The correct action was zero lines of defensive code** вЂ” but the answer came from the source, not from the comment that happened to be nearest.

**A CAPABILITY DELIBERATELY NOT LOST.** Owner-gating the *viewer* does not stop the *capture*: `installCapture()` still runs in `main.tsx` **before React**, so the pre-login trail вЂ” including the customer QR page вЂ” is still **recorded**. David signs in to read it. Worth stating because "we gated debug" reads like "we lost pre-login debugging," and we didn't.

**PROOFS.** `npm run verify` exit 0, **zero net-new** (tsc 5/5 В· eslint 248 В· knip 10/14/15). `build:cultivar` exit 0 **both branches** вЂ” with a SHA the bundle carries the 7-char prefix; without, the literal `"dev"`. Deploy `4eebb08` **confirmed live by fetching the bundle** (`index-DIrngk_f.js`) вЂ” and the poll again caught the stale window: two probes served the old bundle before the third flipped. **Structural claim verified, not asserted:** only `AppLayout` imports the panels, and `AppLayout` sits inside `<PrivateRoute>` (router.tsx:109-110).

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** the 5 owner-test cards вЂ” **card в‘Ў is the leak card** (signed out on `/plant/SCV-0031`, `?debug=1` and `?rhythm=1` must do nothing). **(b)** tech-debt **#60 closes on your live match**, not before. **(c) SCOPE, flagged not accepted:** the rhythm logger records **your** location and voice notes, so an OWNER gate is **wider than its real audience** вЂ” Lauren would see it. The narrower gate needs a platform-dev role that does not exist. **(d)** the recon's other findings are **untouched and still open** вЂ” `/discovery/inspect` is still unauthenticated, and its `send` branch still takes an arbitrary recipient and body with no caller gate. **That is a backend fix; this build did not address it.** **(e)** #56/#59/#61/#62/#63 unchanged.

<!-- Archived 2026-07-21 by the D-52 build close-out (ledger #144) вЂ” В§3 RETENTION N=3 (OP-13).
     Moved VERBATIM from CLAUDE.md В§3, not summarized. entries-in == entries-out: 1 archived, 1 written. -->

### 2026-07-20 вЂ” THUNDER SHA STAMP: the deployed bundle now STATES ITS OWN COMMIT вЂ” OP-15's mechanical form, built in 2 lines of app code. BUILDER-COMPLETE; **owner-prove OWED and tech-debt #60 deliberately NOT closed.** Zero api-fn (12/12), zero schema, zero migration.

**Type:** App code вЂ” 3 files touched, **2 functional lines**: `packages/cultivar-os/vite.config.ts` (NEW `define` block injecting `__COMMIT_SHA__`) В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/components/DebugPanel.tsx` (footer renders `SHA: {__COMMIT_SHA__}` + widget-header OUTPUTS updated per В§9 gate 8) В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/vite-env.d.ts` (`declare const __COMMIT_SHA__: string`). Docs: `docs/tech-debt-log.md` #60 В· `docs/built-inventory.md` (DebugPanel entry + line 4) В· `docs/CLOSE-OUT-LEDGER.md` row #141 В· `docs/owner-tests/inventory-full-surface-test.md` (**GATE 0 в‘  rewritten** + NEW card) В· `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` (OP-15 row + drift line) В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ В· this В§3 + line 3. **В§3 RETENTION: 1 entry archived verbatim (2026-07-17 OP-15), 1 written вЂ” entries-in == entries-out, В§3 back to 3.** **NO new decision** (this IMPLEMENTS OP-15). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** most N/A and stated as such; applicable вЂ” **7 FUNCTION THRIFT** (a build-time string substitution mints ZERO api-fn; 12/12 untouched вЂ” the ceiling was never approached), **9 TRACE STAYS ON** (nothing commented out; the stamp is itself a permanent deploy-provenance signal), **11 OWNER-TEST** (card shipped WITH the surface, set `owed`).

**THE BUILD, exactly.** `define: { __COMMIT_SHA__: JSON.stringify((process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA || 'dev').slice(0,7)) }` в†’ footer `SHA: {__COMMIT_SHA__}` в†’ the global declared for tsc in `vite-env.d.ts`. **Placement (a) вЂ” the DebugPanel footer, per the recon's two options and my lean:** it is where David already stands during an owner-prove, and it keeps Lauren's demo surface clean. (b), an always-visible corner stamp, is a one-line move if David rules otherwise. **Name collision checked, not assumed** вЂ” `__COMMIT_SHA__` appears nowhere in the repo; `src/vite-env.d.ts` was already inside tsconfig's `include`, so the declaration needed no config change.

**PROVEN AT THE BUNDLE, BOTH BRANCHES вЂ” because "the define is right" is a claim until you grep the artifact.** Built with `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA=abc1234deadbeef` в†’ the emitted JS contains **`abc1234`** and **`deadbeef` is ABSENT (count 0)** вЂ” the `slice(0,7)` truncation is real and the full SHA does not leak into a client bundle. Built with **no** env var в†’ the bundle carries the literal **`"dev"`**. `npm run verify` **exit 0, tsc 5/5 вЂ” zero net-new**, which was the one real risk this build carried: an undeclared build-time global is exactly the shape tsc rejects, and the `declare const` held. `build:cultivar` exit 0 both times.

**вљ пёЏ WHY tech-debt #60 IS STILL рџџЎ AND I DID NOT CLOSE IT вЂ” the point of the whole build, applied to the build itself.** The prompt's close-out said flip #60 to RESOLVED *"only after David's live match"*, and that condition has not happened. **A stamp nobody has read on the real phone is a claim** вЂ” and a stamp that is subtly WRONG (stale, wrong var, wrong slice) would not fail loudly; it would **silently un-verify every future GATE 0**, which is precisely the failure class #60 exists to catch. Closing it on a builder's bundle-grep would be the builder grading their own homework (OP-4's two bars) on the very row written because an artifact could not be trusted to say what it was. So: **BUILDER-COMPLETE stated plainly, #60 held OPEN, the owner-test card set `owed` вЂ” Thunder never sets `covered`.**

**GATE 0 CHANGED SHAPE, and that is the deliverable вЂ” not the two lines.** The board's step в‘  was *"in the Vercel dashboard, find the deployment for the SHA under test."* It now reads: **open `?debug=1` в†’ рџђћ в†’ the footer's 7-char SHA must MATCH the pushed commit** вЂ” with `dev` and *blank* called out as distinct, meaningful failures (a local build reached the deploy В· a **pre-stamp bundle**, which is itself proof of old code вЂ” *the stamp catches its own absence*). **The card asserts a MATCH against `git log -1`, deliberately NOT a literal SHA frozen in the doc** вЂ” a hardcoded value goes stale on the very next push, which is the exact rot OP-15 was written to prevent. **OP-15 is NOT retired by this and I did not narrow it:** the panel proves only what the BROWSER holds, so the hard-refresh step stands and the dashboard remains the fallback when the panel is unreachable. The discipline rule and the mechanism now back each other.

**THE THROUGH-LINE THIS CLOSES (2026-07-17's own finding, one layer at a time).** *The artifact does not carry its own provenance* вЂ” `business_inventory` rows could not say who changed them (**closed by D-50's ledger, 2026-07-20**) В· a doctrine corpus could not say what was in it (**closed by the capture-index sweep**) В· **and a deployed bundle could not say what it was вЂ” closed here.** Three layers, one disease, three mechanisms.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** the live match вЂ” `?debug=1` в†’ рџђћ в†’ footer SHA vs `git log -1 --format=%h`; that one glance closes #60. **(b)** placement (b), the always-visible corner stamp, remains your call вЂ” one line. **(c)** the stamp is **cultivar-os only**; `trace-app` and `ignition-os` have their own vite configs and no stamp вЂ” deliberate scope, named not swept. **(d)** tech-debt **#56** / **#59** / **#61** / **#62** / **#63** remain open, unchanged.

### 2026-07-20 вЂ” THUNDER D-50 LAYER 1 вњ… OWNER-PROVEN (David, live at postgres, V1вЂ“V6 all green) вЂ” the movement ledger EXISTS, is DB-IMMUTABLE, and `audit_log` RECEIVED ITS FIRST APPLICATION-AUTHORED ROW after 27 days empty. Close-out is DOCS-ONLY: zero app code, zero new schema, zero migration, zero api-fn (12/12).

**Type:** Close-out write-back вЂ” 6 EDIT (`docs/built-inventory.md` В· `docs/CLOSE-OUT-LEDGER.md` row #140 NEW + row **19B updated** В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` вљЎ ACTIVE STATUS рџ”ґв†’рџџЎ В· `docs/owner-tests/inventory-full-surface-test.md` NEW `SURFACE: movement-ledger` (6 cards, V1вЂ“V6, `covered` by David's live run) В· `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` D-50 row + drift line В· this В§3 + line 3) + 1 ARCHIVE (В§3 overflow). **No `.ts`/`.tsx` opened to edit.** `npm run verify` NOT run вЂ” the gate is code-scoped and no code changed. **NO new decision** (D-50 was already ACCEPTED; this proves Layer 1 of it). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (docs only вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** most N/A and stated as such; applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (D-50's story, `user_stories.md`, cited not re-derived), **11 OWNER-TEST** (this write-back IS the OP-14 gate firing вЂ” David's live run flips the cards; Thunder never sets `covered`).

**OWNER-PROVEN вЂ” 2026-07-20, David, live at postgres on `bgobkjcopcxusjsetfob`, migration `20260720_inventory_movement_ledger.sql` APPLIED.** All six checks green, none asserted: **V1** schema exact + FKs (`business_id` CASCADE, `inventory_id` **SET NULL** вЂ” history outlives the row). **V2** immutability REAL: INSERT ok, **UPDATE and DELETE both rejected** by `reject_inventory_ledger_mutation` вЂ” *and the trigger fires even for `postgres`*, which is the whole point (it defends against our own future code, not just against users). **V3(a)** 126 lots / 126 `opening_balance` rows, exactly one each; **V3(b)** `qty == SUM(delta)`, **zero drift** вЂ” D-50's disagreement #1 ("replay vs on-hand must be ZERO **by construction**") is now a measured fact, not a hope. **V4** RLS `owner_all` OR `member_all`, FOR ALL (so SELECT is covered вЂ” the missing-SELECT trap that bit this platform three times), no `USING(true)`. **V5a** owner adjust 37в†’35 with a ledger row `delta -2`; **F3** a non-owner **MANAGER** passed 35в†’34 `delta -1`; **V5b** forgery (all-zero uuid) RAISED; a cross-tenant actor also correctly refused. **V6** soft-delete: the lot **SURVIVES as a tombstone** (status flipped, qty 0 вЂ” no `DELETE FROM` anywhere), a `delete_tombstone` ledger row `delta -44`, **AND** an `audit_log` row `inventory.delete` / `success` / actor `95c1b2e9`.

**вљ пёЏ ROW 19B IS PARTIALLY CLOSED вЂ” AND THE PART THAT IS STILL OPEN IS THE PART THE RECON RANKED FIRST.** `audit_log` has its first application-authored row. It was created 2026-06-23, owner-proven 2026-06-24, and sat **EMPTY for 27 days** вЂ” immutable, correct, catalog-proven, and never written to, because no writer was ever built. That is closed. **But the writer the spine recon named FIRST вЂ” `deleteTenantRole` в†’ `role.factory_reset` вЂ” is STILL OWED.** This build filled the vault with `inventory.delete`, which was the writer D-50 needed, not the writer 19B specified. Recording the distinction rather than letting "19B: audit_log first writer LANDED" read as if the whole row closed вЂ” **the vault being non-empty is not the same fact as the governance writer existing**, and conflating them is exactly how a row rots while looking done.

**вљ пёЏ THE MID-SESSION FINDING THAT WAS *NOT* A BUG вЂ” and did not become a migration.** V5a first failed: `assert_movement_actor` refused the uid David passed. The prompt diagnosed it as a membership-only check missing the owner branch and specified a corrective migration. **Reading the code first showed the owner branch was already there** вЂ” `is_member_of` is `EXISTS(businesses WHERE owner_id = p_user_id) OR EXISTS(business_members вЂ¦ active)`, already the exact shape prescribed, so the "fix" would have been **logically identical to what was live: a no-op migration**, and F1 would have failed again. Settled by querying live read-only: business `f7ec5d67` "Test Dave's Tree Nest" is owned by **`95c1b2e9`**; the uid under test (`98f4e56b`) owns a **different** business (`ed2e5933` "Test Lawns Tree Farm") and has no member row on `f7ec5d67`; the cited lot `a00a477eвЂ¦` **exists in no tenant at all**. **The gate was refusing a genuine cross-tenant actor вЂ” AC-3 working.** Re-run with the right owner: passed. **The lesson is the one this platform keeps relearning from the other side: a red test is a claim too.** Verify the diagnosis against the code before shipping the fix, or you ship ceremony and stay blocked. Nothing was committed for this; the correct action was zero lines.

**LAYER 2 IS OWED AND NOTHING PRETENDS OTHERWISE.** The DB layer is live and proven; **the app still writes qty the old way.** All ten app-side callers (count SET, desk grid, editor, populate/clear) are UNCHANGED and do NOT yet route through the RPCs вЂ” so today a count still overwrites rather than reconciles, and the recon's live warning (`InventoryCount.tsx:612-614` вЂ” *"count record SKIPPED вЂ¦ inventory already updated"*) still stands. The вљЎ line is **рџџЎ, not рџџў**, for exactly that reason, and the count-loop UI owner-test cards stay **`owed`** вЂ” the SETв†’reconcile rewrite has not shipped.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** the `role.factory_reset` writer (19B's named first writer) remains owed. **(b)** Layer 2 вЂ” rewire the ten callers; the RPCs were built backward-compatible so this can be staged. **(c)** the V2 probe row is permanent and intentional вЂ” cleaning it up would need exactly the power the table denies. **(d)** tech-debt **#56** / **#59** / **#60**'s SHA-stamp remain open, unchanged.

### 2026-07-17 вЂ” THUNDER OP-14 RATIFIED + THE WHOLE CAPTURE-INDEX SWEEP вЂ” and the finding that named the disease of the whole day: a doctrine corpus that cannot say what is in it. DOCS-ONLY: zero app code, zero schema, zero migration, zero api-fn (12/12).

**Type:** Docs/doctrine вЂ” `docs/DECISIONS.md` (OP-14 Status в†’ вњ… RATIFIED) + `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` (OP-14 В§5 row + drift line + Last updated) + `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` (**21 CAPTURE INDEX rows added**, `*(missing)*` placeholder removed, D-42+ sub-table) + `docs/tech-debt-log.md` (#63 NEW) + `docs/CLOSE-OUT-LEDGER.md` #139 + this В§3 + line 3. **ZERO app code / schema / migration / api-fn (12/12).** `npm run verify` NOT run (no code). **NO new decision** (OP-14 ratified unchanged; **OP-15 said docs-only в†’ NO GATE 0 applies, stated not performed**). **В§1.6:** most N/A and stated as such; applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (process/doctrine, no user story, same class as OP-13/OP-15), **8 REUSE** (the sweep reuses the existing index shape; the finding reuses the reconcile-gate pattern). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (docs only вЂ” no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10).

**OP-14 RATIFIED (David, unchanged from proposed).** Flipped in `DECISIONS.md` + `DECISIONS-INDEX` В§5 (#136 proposed в†’ #139 ratified). **Why it needed the explicit call and didn't slide through:** Thunder had FOLLOWED it all day вЂ” wrote the standing board, set `owed`, never set `covered`, waited for David's live run; the four #135 cards flipped only after he ran them. **That is the rule working вЂ” but it was holding by HABIT, not by force.** A gate that lists itself beside the ratified BUILT-INVENTORY / В§3-RETENTION / close-out-ledger / вљЎ-ACTIVE-STATUS gates while itself unratified is **a claim, not a capability вЂ” which is OP-14's own sentence, pointed at OP-14.**

**вљ пёЏ THE FINDING вЂ” RECORD IT, IT IS THE POINT OF THIS PASS.** At 08:00 David asked Lightning what its humor style was. Lightning answered *"not encoded anywhere вЂ” not in CLAUDE.md, not in my corpus,"* and offered to make it an AC-4 candidate. **It was OP-2. Dated 2026-06-03. Status Active/canonical. It names Scott Morrison, the dry edge, BY NAME.** Lightning did not reconstruct it wrong вЂ” it reported it **ABSENT**, from a retrieval structure with a hole exactly there: OP-2 is `[POINTER]`, its content lives in the partnership doc, and **it had no CAPTURE INDEX row.** The index that exists to answer *"where is X"* answered *"nothing here"* вЂ” with the same confidence it answers correctly. **THROUGH-LINE OF 2026-07-17, worth more than any single fix: the artifact does not carry its own provenance** вЂ” `business_inventory` rows cannot say who changed them В· a deployed bundle cannot say what it is (#60/OP-15) В· **and a doctrine corpus cannot say what is in it.** Same disease, three layers.

**THE SWEEP (the count IS the artifact вЂ” a retrieval table with holes is worse than no table).** OP-2/3/14 were found last session *incidentally* while adding OP-11's and OP-15's rows вЂ” that is not a sweep. This pass reconciled the **WHOLE** index against its sources: **59 canonical decisions** (15 OP + 37 `DECISIONS.md` D-headings + 7 `docs/decisions/`-homed D-42вЂ¦D-49). **38 present В· 21 MISSING В· 0 stale** (every referenced file path resolves вЂ” checked). **All 21 filled:** OP-2, OP-3, OP-14 В· D-27вЂ¦D-36, D-40 В· D-42/43/45/46/47/48/49. The last 7 got a **new sub-table** because they live *outside* `DECISIONS.md` вЂ” itself a flagged gap (fold them in). OP-3's row names only the framework + that its criteria are personal-sensitive and live outside the repo вЂ” **it does not hint at the criteria**, deliberately.

**tech-debt #63 вЂ” THE CAPTURE INDEX HAS NO GATE.** Every other canonical surface has a binding reconciliation gate; the index that makes them all *findable* is maintained by a step-6 **reminder** in `end-of-session-protocol.md` вЂ” and **a reminder is what rots** (row-19B: detection produces a note; notes don't act). **NOT proposed as an OP this pass** вЂ” David has ratified two OPs in two days; a third now is ceremony. Named, numbered, left to **earn** its gate when the pattern recurs.

**NO OWNER-PROVE OWED** (docs). **built-inventory.md: N/A вЂ” nothing built/changed** (doctrine + an index reconcile), stated rather than skipped. The sweep proves itself the next time someone asks *"where is X"* and the index answers correctly.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a)** tech-debt **#63** вЂ” the CAPTURE INDEX wants a gate; left to earn it. **(b)** the 7 D-42вЂ¦D-49 decisions live in `docs/decisions/`, not `DECISIONS.md` вЂ” a fold-in pass would single-home the OP/D mirror. **(c)** the humor-style itself is now *findable* (OP-2 has a row) but still lives only in the partnership doc вЂ” if you want it in the always-loaded corpus, that is a separate AC-4 call. **(d)** tech-debt **#56** / **#59** remain open, unchanged.

### 2026-07-17 вЂ” THUNDER OP-15 RATIFIED: owner-prove STEP ZERO вЂ” confirm the deploy for THIS SHA is READY before any observation is evidence вЂ” and the homing IS the deliverable (the board, where David stands, not a protocol doc). DOCS-ONLY: zero app code, zero schema, zero migration, zero api-fn (12/12).

**Type:** Docs/doctrine вЂ” 1 NEW GATE (`docs/operating-doctrine/end-of-session-protocol.md` в†’ GATE вЂ” DEPLOYED / OWNER-PROVE STEP ZERO) + `docs/DECISIONS.md` OP-15 + `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` (OP-15 В§5 row + OP-1вЂ¦OP-15 header + drift line + Last updated) + `docs/owner-tests/inventory-full-surface-test.md` (GATE 0 strengthened to OP-15) + `CLAUDE.md` В§9 (NEW standing instruction + DEPLOYED-bar STEP ZERO) + В§4 tech-debt quick-ref + В§3 + line 3 + `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md` (CAPTURE INDEX OP-11 + OP-15 rows + header) + `docs/tech-debt-log.md` (#60 в†’ RATIFIED) + **2 RELOCATED** (`data/grower-scan/audit-{event-model,writer-gate}-recon.md` в†’ `docs/decisions/2026-07-17-audit-*.md`) + **1 DELETED** (`.git-commit-msg` scratch). **ZERO app code** вЂ” no `.ts`/`.tsx` EDITED (vite.config.ts + DebugPanel.tsx READ for the recon only). `npm run verify` NOT run (no code changed). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (docs only вЂ” no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). **В§1.6:** most items N/A and stated as such; applicable вЂ” **1 STORY** (process/doctrine, no user story required, same class as OP-13/OP-14/ledger #134), **10 THREE-LENS** (the SHA-stamp recon is HAVE/NEED/WANT, report-only).

**THE DECISION:** owner-prove **STEP ZERO = confirm the deployment for THIS SHA is READY** вЂ” before the hard-refresh, before the bundle-hash check, before any screen is read as evidence. If the SHA under test is not live, **every observation after that is fiction.** A failed deploy is SILENT (Vercel serves last-good), and **Vercel deploys the TREE, not the COMMIT** вЂ” *you can ship code by writing a document, and fail to ship code by pushing it.* **Number CONFIRMED not guessed:** OP-1вЂ¦OP-14 in both `DECISIONS.md` headings AND the `DECISIONS-INDEX` В§5 table в‡’ **OP-15**.

**вљ пёЏ THE HOMING WAS THE DELIVERABLE, NOT A DETAIL вЂ” and it is why this is an OP and not another note.** OP-13 was homed in `end-of-session-protocol.md` + a В§9 pointer, correctly, because **Thunder** is its actor and reads those. **OP-15's actor is DAVID at a screen, who does NOT read the protocol mid-test.** A rule filed where the actor isn't standing is **row 19B** вЂ” detected, noted, marked UNBLOCKEDвЂ“NOT STARTED, and it sat 23 days; detection produced a note, and notes don't act. The proof that writing it harder fails: *"confirm Vercel READY"* was in every Thunder prompt all week, in the right words, and **ran for the first time only today because a build failure forced it** вЂ” #135 sat dead ~20h. So it is homed **where the actor stands: (a) PRIMARY** вЂ” a strengthened **GATE 0 block at the TOP of the inventory owner-test board** (SHA line first, *"if the SHA isn't live, nothing below is evidence"*); **(b) SECONDARY** вЂ” CLAUDE.md В§9 standing instruction + DEPLOYED-bar, so Thunder carries it; **(c)** the OP + a sibling GATE section as the binding statement. **(a) OUTRANKS (c) вЂ” stated because it is the whole point:** a future session that files this only in a protocol doc and calls it done has failed the way row 19B failed. **вљ пёЏ Only ONE owner-test board exists** (`inventory-full-surface-test.md`); the "orders board" cited in ledger #136 is Lightning's, not in `docs/owner-tests/` вЂ” the standing rule (OP-15 + В§9) means any future board, the planned orders one included, inherits GATE 0.

**RECON вЂ” THE SHA STAMP (report-only, David rules the build; FLAGGED not ratified).** OP-15 is still a *discipline* rule вЂ” it depends on a human checking a second system (the Vercel dashboard). The mechanical form stamps the git SHA into the app at build and renders it in-app, so GATE 0 becomes *"does the app say `313de44`?"* вЂ” one glance, no dashboard. It collapses TWO identically-presenting scars into one tell: stale bundle (#128/#129 в†’ old JS carries the old SHA) and failed deploy (#60 в†’ server serves the old bundle). Same disease this session found in `business_inventory` (rows can't say who changed them): **the artifact doesn't carry its own provenance.** Findings, `file:line`:
- **HAVE:** `packages/cultivar-os/vite.config.ts` has **no `define` block** ([vite.config.ts:5](packages/cultivar-os/vite.config.ts#L5)) вЂ” `VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA` (exposed by Vercel at build) is **not injected**. A render surface **already exists**: `DebugPanel` (the `?debug=1` рџђћ panel; footer at [DebugPanel.tsx:110](packages/cultivar-os/src/components/DebugPanel.tsx#L110)) вЂ” **so the SHA is a FIELD on an existing panel, not a new component.**
- **NEED (smallest that works):** one `define` line (`__COMMIT_SHA__: JSON.stringify((process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA||'dev').slice(0,7))`) + one render line in the DebugPanel footer. **Zero new api-fn** (build-time define + a string вЂ” 12/12 ceiling untouched). GATE 0 becomes "does the panel show the SHA under test?"
- **WANT:** an always-visible corner stamp (findable without enabling debug) вЂ” but that risks demo clutter for Lauren; the DebugPanel ride is proportionate because owner-proves already enable it. **David rules which.**
- **вЏ± 30 MINUTES, not an afternoon** (one config line + one render line + confirming the env var resolves on Vercel). **Demo-week-safe.**

**HOUSEKEEPING (David ruled):** the two untracked recon files were **committed, relocated** from `data/grower-scan/` to `docs/decisions/2026-07-17-audit-{event-model,writer-gate}-recon.md` (beside `2026-07-17-inventory-reconcile-confidence-recon.md`). **Why they were in a data folder:** `data/grower-scan/` is an established recon-family folder (member-rls, role-machine, discovery, `audit-spine-recon.md` all live there), so these two followed the local sibling вЂ” but the audit-vault recon trio belongs together where the decision will mint. The `audit-spine-recon.md` sibling stays put; the one cross-ref between the moved pair was repointed. Scratch `.git-commit-msg` deleted.

**CAPTURE INDEX (STD-011) вЂ” OP-11 had no row (David flagged) вЂ” ADDED, plus OP-15.** A capture without an index row is "not done" (bootstrap В§202). **Also absent: OP-2, OP-3, OP-14 вЂ” FLAGGED not silently swept** (a `*(missing)*` row names them for the next sweep).

**NO OWNER-PROVE OWED вЂ” and the reason is the point (same as OP-13/OP-14).** OP-15 is process scaffold: no UI, no RLS path, no live behavior. **It proves itself on the NEXT owner-prove** вЂ” if David reads a SHA before testing, the homing worked; if he tests old code again, it failed, visibly. **The gate is its own test.** The SHA-stamp BUILD is the only owed follow-on, and it is David's call.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a) rule the SHA-stamp build** вЂ” 30 min, demo-week-safe, zero new api-fn; the durable form of OP-15. **(b)** OP-2/OP-3/OP-14 still need CAPTURE-INDEX rows (named, not swept). **(c)** tech-debt **#56** (size vocabulary), **#59** (bootstrap header) remain open, unchanged. **(d)** the two audit-vault recons (event-model, writer-gate) now sit in `docs/decisions/` awaiting your ruling on the audit build (writer-gate was ranked ABOVE O1).

This is the input that minted Acoma's CASE 5 twin yesterday.

Filtered to `alley` (4/123, sizes 15/30/45/60 all distinct): *"2 flagged rows **elsewhere** вЂ¦ nothing on this screen is affected."* Two different sentences by what is in view; yesterday it said "2 size collisions вЂ” edit a flagged row" over four clean rows. Copy nit fixed too: "2 flagged **rows**" agrees with the trace's `Array(1)` (one collision, two rows). *(Flipped board cards: blank-size В· add-size dup В· suffixed-sibling SKU В· clean-filter-not-here В· banner-fires-here В· count-noun-agrees вЂ” six granular cards, the four proof-cards mapped one-to-many onto the by-surface board.)*

**вљ пёЏ THE DEPLOY FINDING вЂ” BIGGER THAN THE BUILD, and it is a NEW bar-of-the-gap, not the #128/#129 scar.** **`313de44` never deployed вЂ” its Vercel build FAILED** (18:59:43 start в†’ 18:59:45 clone в†’ *"Build Failed вЂ” an unexpected error вЂ¦ may be a transient issue"*, dying AFTER clone and BEFORE install: no npm, no tsc, Vercel infrastructure not our code). **#135 went live only as a SIDE EFFECT of deploying a markdown file:** the most-recent GREEN deploy is **`77ffd8e`** (the #137 recon DOC, ~15:37 today), and since git is cumulative, `77ffd8e`'s tree CONTAINS `313de44` вЂ” **VERIFIED, not inferred:** `git merge-base --is-ancestor 313de44 77ffd8e` в†’ **CONFIRMED**. It explains this morning's mystery: at 09:34 the banner read "2 size collisions" over 8 clean oaks вЂ” not a stale bundle, not a missed fix, but **CORRECT behavior for the code actually deployed** (#135 was never live). **The lesson is one layer deeper than #128/#129:** that scar taught "committed в‰  live" в†’ *hard-refresh, check the bundle hash* (a browser problem). This is **"the bundle cannot be stale if it was never built."** DEPLOYED is a bar nobody measures вЂ” Thunder's last act is push, David's first is test, **nothing between them confirms the build succeeded**, and #135 sat dead ~20 hours while everyone assumed it shipped. **And Vercel deploys the TREE, not the COMMIT** вЂ” any push carries every unshipped commit beneath it, so *you can ship code by writing a document, and fail to ship code by pushing it.* Recorded as **tech-debt #60** with a **PROPOSED (not taken) standing rule: owner-prove STEP ZERO = "confirm the Vercel deployment for THIS SHA is READY" вЂ” before the hard-refresh, before the bundle-hash check.** Ten seconds. Every Thunder prompt already carried "confirm Vercel READY" in its deploy-bar section; today was the first time all day it was run. Per row-19B's lesson, a В§1.6 checklist item alone will ROT вЂ” if David ratifies, it belongs in the close-out protocol with gate-force, said so in #60.

**RECORD, DO NOT BUILD (findings this session; all in tech-debt or flagged, none acted on):**
- **(a) tech-debt #61 вЂ” `countPromote.ts:24` comment is FALSE.** It asserts scrape-reads-variations "was never built"; the #137 recon proved `fetchProductVariants`/`extractSizeVariants` EXIST and are wired into `populateCatalog` (git: added 2026-06-28 / 2026-06-30). **A comment contradicting its own repo fed two days of wrong reasoning** (Lightning repeated it as ground truth in the D-49 prompt). Comment-only fix, NOT this docs pass.
- **(b) the scrape has never re-run вЂ” SETTLED with the git date the prompt asked for.** Live: 116 DISC rows, 111 written in ONE burst 2026-06-26 17:32, 5 by David's hands 2026-07-16; only 11 carry a size, each a singleton, in mixed vocabulary вЂ” which one Woo parser cannot produce from one write. **The enrichment landed AFTER the only scrape:** `catalog.ts` created 2026-06-21, but `fetchProductVariants` (2026-06-28) and `extractSizeVariants` (2026-06-30) both postdate the 06-26 17:32 write вЂ” so the live sizes cannot have come from the enrichment, confirming no scrape has run since. **Consequence: the catalog cleanup is GATED on a David ruling** вЂ” `populate` CLEARS prior discovery, so a re-run wipes every DISC- row (the four orphan pairs, Acoma's twin, `DISC-1003-30G-45G`, Sierra's SKU-less 30 gal) AND nulls `inventory_counts.inventory_id` via the FK. **The stale #133 fold SQL may be MOOT if David rules "re-scrape."** Do NOT re-run anything. (Related: #56, #57.)
- **(c) `counted_by` is 28/28 populated** (live, David ran it) вЂ” the walk's actor is real, at session grain, one join away. **Any note implying "no counter identity" is wrong** вЂ” correcting the record.
- **(d) `supa_audit` is a real, tested Supabase table-auditing extension** (github.com/supabase/supa_audit вЂ” trigger-based, JSONB `record`/`old_record`, per-table opt-in, auto-adds `auth_uid`/`auth_role` from `auth.uid()`). It is largely the "build a trigger" slice of the audit plan, already written. **The catch that shapes the design:** `submit.ts` resolves the caller via `resolveCallerUid(authHeader)` вЂ” which builds an **ANON-key client carrying the caller's Bearer token** (`packages/shared/src/auth/callerPermission.ts:89-97`), **not** a service-role client вЂ” and then hand-carries that uid as a VALUE into writes that execute on `adminDb()` (the service key). So `auth.uid()` is NULL for server writes because of where the WRITE runs, not because of how the caller was resolved. Client-direct writes (grid edit, tier dropdown) get the actor free, but **server writes need an app-level event** вЂ” which is *why* industry runs both layers. вљ пёЏ **Mechanism sentence corrected 2026-07-20** (D-50 Layer 1; the original said "в†’ a service-role client", which is the sentence a future session would design from вЂ” same class as tech-debt #61, a note contradicting its own repo). The conclusion is unchanged. **Verify availability in `bgobkjcopcxusjsetfob` before anyone plans around it** вЂ” do not take it from Lightning. (Reference for future audit work вЂ” `docs/decisions/2026-07-17-audit-event-model-recon.md` + `docs/decisions/2026-07-17-audit-writer-gate-recon.md`; they were relocated there 2026-07-17 and are no longer the "untracked `data/grower-scan/`" files this line used to name.)
- **(e) tech-debt #62 вЂ” the DataSheet viewport standard DRIFTED (one item, both halves).** The horizontal scrollbar is anchored to the bottom of TABLE CONTENT, not a fixed-height scroll viewport вЂ” at 123 rows it is 123 rows down. **Worse half:** SELL PRICE and CONF are past the RIGHT fold (NAME/ACTIONS/SKU/QTY/SIZE/VARIANT GRP/UNIT COST all outrank them) вЂ” the column that decides whether a row can be sold, cut off on the surface whose own header calls it "board 5.1 reconcile surface," on the day the finding was that Bur Oak looks finished and isn't. David's point: **a set convention drifted, not a missing pixel** вЂ” inventory is the first DataSheet consumer long enough to surface it. Recon question recorded in #62: does the fixed-height viewport live in `DataSheet.tsx` or per-page, and do assets/customers pin while inventory doesn't?

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built):** **(a) RATIFY tech-debt #60's proposed rule** вЂ” owner-prove step ZERO = confirm Vercel READY for THIS SHA; if yes, it wants close-out-protocol force, not a rot-prone В§1.6 line. **(b)** the catalog-cleanup ruling (finding b) вЂ” **re-scrape vs regenerate-the-fold** вЂ” decide with the 06-26-vs-06-28 date in front of you; the #133 fold stays STALE/unapplied until you rule. **(c)** two untracked recon files sit in `git status` вЂ” `data/grower-scan/audit-event-model-recon.md` + `audit-writer-gate-recon.md` (plus a scratch `.git-commit-msg`) вЂ” surfaced per the session health check; **not swept into this commit** (not this session's work вЂ” your call whether they're kept). **(d)** tech-debt **#56** (size vocabulary) and **#59** (bootstrap header) remain open from the #135/#134 family, unchanged.
