### 2026-07-17 вЂ” THUNDER RECON: THE COUNT RECORD, THE CONFIDENCE PRIMITIVE, AND RECONCILE-TO-SELLABLE (ledger #137): nobody had opened `inventory_counts` вЂ” the model was being designed blind. Look-then-decide. The finding: **the pending-capture queue ALREADY EXISTS as rows and NOTHING reads it**; the reconcile "build" is largely a READER + a shared predicate + a grid filter, NOT a new store. REPORT ONLY вЂ” zero code, zero schema, zero migration, zero api-fn (12/12)

**Type:** RECON document only вЂ” 1 NEW (`docs/decisions/2026-07-17-inventory-reconcile-confidence-recon.md`, the three-lens recon) + this handoff + ledger #137 + DECISIONS-INDEX drift line. **ZERO app code, ZERO schema, ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12 untouched), ZERO `[TRACE:*]`** вЂ” no `.ts`/`.tsx` opened. `npm run verify` NOT run (nothing built). **NO decision number proposed** (per instruction вЂ” if David rules an option in, that is where a decision mints). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10). Serves `user_stories.md:362` + `:370` (both `STATUS: written`), cited in the doc.

**WHY IT EXISTED:** two hours of reconcile/confidence design had rested on inference вЂ” the table everyone was designing against, `inventory_counts`, had not been opened. Lightning fabricated a trace record the day before and David caught it. This recon is the correction: **look, cite, then decide.** Every claim in the doc carries `file:line`; three claims are labelled BLOCKED (no local service key вЂ” same block D-49 hit) rather than guessed.

**THE HEADLINE, AND IT IS A CORRECTION TO THE MODEL:** **the queue is already built вЂ” as ROWS вЂ” and read by NOTHING.** "Skip & flag" writes `inventory_counts {inventory_id: null, was_unknown: true, item_label, counted_qty, raw_scan}` ([InventoryCount.tsx:545](packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/InventoryCount.tsx#L545)); the typed/ambiguous path writes a labelled row too. But grepping every reader: **there is NO `.from('inventory_counts').select(...)` anywhere** вЂ” the migration itself says reconciliation "is DEFERRED вЂ” this only leaves room for it." So `inventory_counts` is a **write-only audit log**, and the pending-capture store the design keeps describing **is a table that already has rows in it and no screen.** A large part of "build reconcile" is "build a READER."

**THE SMALLEST VERSION THAT WORKS (David's own question вЂ” is it over-designed? Partly yes):**
- **NEED = a shared sellable predicate + a grid filter, no migration.** The refusal that stopped Chinkapin (`sell_price == null || sell_price <= 0`) is **inline and duplicated 3Г—** ([submit.ts:360](packages/cultivar-os/api/orders/submit.ts#L360), [:914](packages/cultivar-os/api/orders/submit.ts#L914), [CartReview.tsx:131](packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/CartReview.tsx#L131)) with **no shared function** вЂ” so showing "not sellable yet" on the grid would fork a **4th copy at the money surface**. Extract it to ONE shared predicate FIRST (the keystone); then a `/inventory` filter over it + "never counted" (derived from the already-written `inventory_counts`) + "still a stub" (`isVarietyStub` already exists). **The reconcile SURFACE is the grid** вЂ” `BusinessInventory.tsx` header literally says "board 5.1 reconcile surface"; it needs two derived columns and a filter, not a new screen.
- **ALREADY BUILT, that David may think needs building:** the unknown/pending queue (rows exist, unread), resolve-before-create (built at [InventoryCount.tsx:576](packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/InventoryCount.tsx#L576), `owed`/unproven not missing), the reconcile surface (the grid), and **size-scraping** (`fetchProductVariants` reads Woo variation sizes and writes per-size rows TODAY вЂ” the 103 live size-less stubs are **stale data predating the enrichment**, not a missing feature).
- **OVER-designed for today's pain:** `qty_as_of` as a NEW column (currency can be *derived* from the unread table вЂ” WANT not NEED); a qty/identity **confidence ladder** (real for the *spoken walk* `:376`, not for the typed/scan path staring at Bur Oak/Chinkapin whose qty is a firm count); **voice** (a separate build with an infra decision in front of it).

**THREE FINDINGS THAT CONSTRAIN ANY BUILD (all cited in the doc):**
- **AC-1 / confidence:** `cost_confidence` is a **DB CHECK** (`20260612_..._cost_confidence.sql:58`), and the 4-value ladder is **5 literal CHECK copies across 3 migrations** + the TS type forked in в‰Ґ4 files вЂ” **no shared DB domain, no shared primitive** (STD-011). There is **NO identity/qty/tax confidence ladder at all** (`person_spine` has none). So a new `qty_confidence` would become the **6th CHECK copy + AC-1 debt** unless modelled as **growable free-text** (the discovery-JSONB precedent already does exactly that: "NO CHECK вЂ” the value-set grows without a migration").
- **E вЂ” sizes AND prices are ONE structure, and for LAWNS the price half is empty by nature.** The Woo `data-product_variations` JSON the parser already reads carries `display_price` per variation ([test fixture](packages/shared/src/discovery/catalog-variants.test.ts#L35)) beside the size вЂ” parsed and **discarded** ([catalog.ts:451](packages/shared/src/discovery/catalog.ts#L451)). BUT a read-only fetch of the live LAWNS page shows **sizes, no prices** вЂ” LAWNS is catalog-only wholesale. So scrape-reads-price is **one JSON key** but **useless for LAWNS**: pricing stays **homework (Lauren enters), not review (Lauren approves)**. That makes O1's "needs price" filter *more* important, not less.
- **F вЂ” voice's ceiling-safe path already has a live precedent.** Browser Web Speech API runs in cultivar today ([RhythmLogger.tsx](packages/cultivar-os/src/components/RhythmLogger.tsx), `?rhythm=1`, zero endpoints); the Ignition donor POSTs to an off-repo LAN Whisper. `voice_capture` itself is **not built**, and a server transcriber would be api-fn **#13 = silent deploy failure** (12/12). Voice is a distinct build with an infra decision, not a rider on reconcile.

**THE ORDER (why what before what):** (1) shared sellable predicate FIRST вЂ” the keystone; every grid signal depends on it and building it late forks the money rule. (2) grid reader/filter (reads `inventory_counts`, no migration) вЂ” unblocks the live pain. (3) `qty_as_of` column only if derived-currency proves insufficient. (4) confidence ladder gated on a **free-text-vs-CHECK decision**, and on the spoken-walk being the active story. (5) voice gated on the 12/12 ceiling call. Does scrape-price shrink the reconcile build? **No вЂ” it makes it honestly smaller for LAWNS (sizes recoverable, prices not), which is why "needs price" must be a first-class filter.** Does skip&flag mean the queue exists? **YES, unambiguously.**

**В§1.6:** most items **N/A and stated as such** (no code/schema/UI-control/endpoint/money path changed вЂ” this is a document). Applicable: **1 STORY** (cites `:362` + `:370`, both written), **10 THREE-LENS** (the doc IS HAVE/NEED/WANT + OPTIONS spanning NEEDв†’WANT, no pre-collapsed recommendation вЂ” a recon, per В§9 gate 10). **NO built-inventory touch вЂ” nothing was built, stated here rather than skipped silently.** **NO owner-prove owed** (a document has no live surface to drive).

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built):** **(a)** three BLOCKED items need a live catalog read or a short-lived PAT (are the 103 stubs stale-vs-crawl-failing; does live LAWNS JSON carry `display_price` keys; the full other-ladder sweep is done but two rest on migration reads). **(b)** the confidence ladder's **free-text-vs-CHECK** call is the gate on any qty/identity confidence work вЂ” decide before code or inherit AC-1 debt. **(c)** tech-debt #56 (size vocabulary) and #59 (bootstrap header) remain open from #135/#134; unchanged this session.

<!-- Archived 2026-07-17 from CLAUDE.md В§3 HANDOFF вЂ” the SIXTH application of the N=3 retention rule (OP-13, RATIFIED). ONE entry: OWNER-TEST BOARD + THE COVERAGE GATE (ledger #136, 2026-07-16), which fell out of В§3 when the OP-15 ratification close-out (2026-07-17) was written. Moved VERBATIM вЂ” nothing summarized; extracted from CLAUDE.md lines 400вЂ“424. CLAUDE.md В§3 now retains the newest three: OP-15 RATIFIED (2026-07-17), #135 OWNER-PROVEN (2026-07-17), the inventory reconcile/confidence recon (2026-07-17, ledger #137). Entries in == entries out: 3 in В§3 + 1 new = 4 в†’ 1 archived + 3 kept. Archive 183 в†’ 184. -->

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER OWNER-TEST BOARD + THE COVERAGE GATE (ledger #136, OP-14 proposed): "a surface without a test is a CLAIM, not a capability" вЂ” the two-bar rule already said so and NOTHING made the gap visible; the board's first verdict was on its own author's work вЂ” **19% PROVEN, 4 of 8 surfaces with NO test**; NO app code, ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12)

**Type:** Docs/process + 1 renderer вЂ” 2 NEW (`docs/owner-tests/inventory-full-surface-test.md` the STANDING test В· `owner-tests.html` the PURE renderer) + 6 EDIT (`docs/operating-doctrine/end-of-session-protocol.md` NEW gate В· `CLAUDE.md` В§1.6 item 11 + В§9 standing instruction + close-step 12 + В§3 + line 3 В· `docs/DECISIONS.md` OP-14 В· `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` В· `docs/built-inventory.md` В· `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md`) + **1 DELETED** (`docs/owner-tests/2026-07-16-inventory-count-add-owner-test.md` вЂ” retired into the standing test, STD-011) + ledger #136. **ZERO app code** вЂ” no `.ts`/`.tsx` opened; `npm run verify` NOT run (the gate is code-scoped and no code changed). **ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12), ZERO `[TRACE:*]` touched.** Decision **OP-14 (proposed)** вЂ” number **CONFIRMED, not guessed**: OP-1вЂ¦OP-13 exist in BOTH `DECISIONS.md` headings AND the `DECISIONS-INDEX` В§5 table в‡’ OP-14 is next. **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10).

**THE PROBLEM, IN DAVID'S OWN WORDS:** *"we have had some drift in today's efforts and David is unsure if I can trust the system."* That is the requirement. **OP-4's two bars already said BUILDER-COMPLETE в‰  OWNER-PROVEN вЂ” but nothing made the gap VISIBLE.** An unproven surface rendered identically to a proven one, so the honest answer to *"can I trust this?"* was **a feeling, and the only instrument was whether somebody happened to remember.** And David's second point is the one that makes it a GATE and not a doc: **testing is tedious, so it must be REPEATABLE** вЂ” a test nobody can re-run is a test nobody trusts, which is how a green board becomes worthless.

**THE SCAR IT ANSWERS вЂ” one day, three fixes, each correct:** #132 unblocked a resolver path в†’ immediately exposed a promote that had never run on it (#133, *counting a variety made it permanently unscannable*) в†’ fixing that exposed three more mint paths that ignored its invariant (#135) вЂ” **all four minted through the UI within the hour.** **Every fix was verified. Every fix was verified on the surface that motivated it. The surface it didn't reach is where the next defect lived.** STD-017 *states* that requirement; this makes it **checkable instead of promised**.

**BUILT вЂ” (1) THE STANDING TEST.** `docs/owner-tests/inventory-full-surface-test.md`: **27 checks across 8 surfaces** (resolve В· count-promote В· typed-entry В· add-size В· manual-crud В· grid В· order-picker В· offline), organized **BY SURFACE** вЂ” *that structure is why the four untested ones became visible at all* (STD-017 made physical). Every card: `STATUS` (`covered`|`owed`|`needs-test`|`blocked`) В· `DEVICE` В· `COVERS:` (ledger #) В· `LAST-PROVEN` В· `SIGNAL` (**always secondary**). **Shape adopted from Lightning's `orders-full-surface-test.md`** вЂ” by-surface bands, a SETUP block, a TRACE appendix, and its excellent closer (*"if a result surprises you, re-check GATE 0 before believing it"*) вЂ” **plus** the reporting discipline the dated script had that his lacks (a results table, what-to-send-back, an explicit blast-radius warning, and cards whose whole job is catching a guard that **OVER**-reaches).

**BUILT вЂ” (2) THE RENDERER.** `owner-tests.html`, the **third sibling** of `status.html`/`stories.html`: parses the .md live at open-time, **holds ZERO data** в†’ **there is no board to regenerate and nothing to drift**. Default view is **"Not proven"** (the honest default: what you cannot trust yet), plus "No test written" and "All"; filters by surface/status/device/**COVERS** (so *"what closes #135"* is a filter, not a document); headline **trust meter**. **В§6 r16 held: a deliberate CLONE of the established `stories.html` pattern** вЂ” same load/parse/render/empty-state/styling вЂ” **not a third invented shape.**

**BUILT вЂ” (3) THE GATE (OP-14).** *A build that ADDS, CHANGES, or POLISHES a surface must add, update, or `needs-test`-mark its card.* Four clauses: **(a)** touch a surface в†’ touch its card, silence is not an option; **(b)** **`needs-test` is a first-class RED value** вЂ” writing the test isn't always this build's job, **recording that it's missing always is** (**D-9 applied to our own confidence**: a known hole is a decision, an unrecorded hole is a lie by omission); **(c)** **a MOVED surface flips `covered` в†’ `owed`** вЂ” *a green check on a moved surface is worse than none, because it asserts a proof nobody performed*, **which is the precise shape of D-49's own suite blessing the defect it was written to prevent**; **(d)** a per-build proof is a **FILTER**, never a second document. **THUNDER MAY NEVER SET `covered`** вЂ” Thunder writes the check and sets `owed`; only David's live run flips it, with a date. **The builder does not grade their own homework** вЂ” this is OP-4 given a scoreboard and the pen handed to David. Homed exactly as its siblings: force in `end-of-session-protocol.md` в†’ **GATE вЂ” OWNER-TEST COVERAGE**; pointers at В§9 + **В§1.6 item 11** + close-step **12**. **Both APPENDED, steps 1вЂ“11 NOT renumbered** вЂ” the OP-13 lesson applied verbatim: В§1.6 item 9 cites *"В§9 gate 9"* and steps 10/11 cite *"item 8"/"item 9"*, so renumbering would silently break four live cross-references to fix a formatting preference.

**вљ пёЏ THE BOARD'S FIRST ACT WAS TO GRADE ITS OWN AUTHOR'S WORK, AND THE NUMBER IS BAD: 19% PROVEN вЂ” 5 of 27 checks. FOUR of eight surfaces ship NO test at all.** **Nobody hid any of this. It was never counted.** The holes, named: **order-picker READ** вЂ” *a counted size actually being sellable*, **the entire reason D-45 exists**, never proven end-to-end (the count half is proven; **the read half is not** вЂ” and "the count promotes" and "the picker shows it" are two surfaces, which is the STD-017 scar exactly). **Offline sync** вЂ” shipped, load-bearing, never proven, **and the lot has dead zones**. **Delete soft/hard** вЂ” money-adjacent: the FKs are `ON DELETE SET NULL`, so a wrong delete **silently blanks a sales line rather than erroring**. **The frozen-column** grid standard (В§6 r14). **That number IS the deliverable** вЂ” it is the first honest answer to "can I trust the system," and it is a number instead of a feeling.

**STD-011 вЂ” I DELETED MY OWN 20-MINUTE-OLD DOC, and the reason is the point.** The dated `2026-07-16-inventory-count-add-owner-test.md` was **retired into the standing test and removed**. Two docs answering *"how do I prove the blank-size guard?"* are two representations of one fact вЂ” **and drift is precisely what makes a test unbelievable**, which is the exact thing David said he'd lost. A per-build proof is now `COVERS: #135` on one board. Keeping both would have been the disease wearing a test's clothes.

**VERIFIED, NOT ASSERTED (the discipline this session was about):** I built a renderer that parses a file, so I **extracted the renderer's OWN parser and ran it against the real .md** rather than eyeballing it: **27 checks В· 8 surfaces В· 0 bad STATUS В· 0 missing tags В· 0 empty bodies В· COVERS resolving to 20 distinct ledger/decision ids.** *A board that silently fails to parse its source is the same defect class as a banner that mis-attributes a collision* вЂ” and I had just spent the day fixing one of those.

**CONSTRAINTS HELD:** **OP-4** (this is its scoreboard) В· **OP-11** (this is its **missing other half**: OP-11 records the VERDICT when David reports owner-proven; OP-14 guarantees a **repeatable way to reach it**) В· **OP-13** (same shape, deliberately вЂ” a rule that rides the close-out ritual rather than needing tooling) В· **STD-017** (the by-surface structure is the enumeration) В· **STD-011** В· **D-9** В· **В§6 r10** (standard-by-value, at the **cheapest form that meets the need**: a markdown file + a one-file pure renderer вЂ” **no test framework, no CI, no coverage tool, deliberately**, because the thing under test is *a human driving a phone in a lot*, which no runner can do; adopting Jest/Playwright here would be the over-engineering trap the rule names) В· **В§6 r16** В· **capture=mobile/reconcile=desktop** (`DEVICE: phone` cards must be provable **WITHOUT a console** вЂ” a check that needs DevTools never gets run in a lot; this is why every PASS is visible and `SIGNAL:` is always secondary). **В§1.6:** most items **N/A and stated as such** (no code/schema/UI-control/endpoint/money path); applicable: **1 STORY** (docs/process infra вЂ” no user story required, same class as ledger #38/#46/#50/#134), **8 REUSE** (the renderer reuses `stories.html`'s pattern; the gate reuses the BUILT-INVENTORY gate's shape and the В§9 standing-instruction pattern вЂ” **no new mechanism invented**), **11 OWNER-TEST** (this build *is* item 11; it adds the board it demands).

**NO OWNER-PROVE OWED FOR THE GATE вЂ” and the reason is the point (same as OP-13).** It is process scaffold: no UI, no RLS path, no live behavior. **It proves itself on the NEXT close-out** вЂ” if a surface-touching build lands without touching its card, the gate failed, **and the board shows it at a glance**. **The gate is its own test.** The 22 `owed`/`needs-test` cards it exposes are owed by their own builds, not by this one.

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID:** **(a) RATIFY OP-14** (proposed). **(b)** The **order-picker READ** hole is the highest-value one on the board and should probably be the next build вЂ” D-45's whole thesis is unproven at its far end. **(c)** Lightning's `orders-full-surface-test.md` should adopt this schema + the renderer (it's already the right shape; it needs the tags) вЂ” **and it has an unflagged blast radius: `QBO_ENVIRONMENT = production`, so every run pushes REAL invoices into TRACE Enterprises' production QuickBooks**, and under BENCH-F a sent invoice is immutable (void + credit note, never delete). **That test leaves permanent artifacts in the same ledger where the nine mis-billed invoices landed, and it doesn't say so.** It should, near the top, with a void procedure. **(d)** Once both tests carry the schema, `owner-tests.html` should load **all** of `docs/owner-tests/` rather than one file вЂ” trivial, deferred until there are two. **(e)** tech-debt **#59** (bootstrap header) still open; **#56** (size vocabulary) is still the only live defect in the inventory family.

<!-- Archived 2026-07-17 from CLAUDE.md В§3 HANDOFF вЂ” the FIFTH application of the N=3 retention rule (OP-13, RATIFIED). ONE entry: THE FOUR WAYS A WORKING VARIETY GOT BROKEN THROUGH THE UI (ledger #135, 2026-07-16, BUILDER-COMPLETE), which fell out of В§3 when its own OWNER-PROVEN close-out (ledger #135 owner-proven, 2026-07-17) was written. Moved VERBATIM вЂ” nothing summarized; extracted from CLAUDE.md lines 403вЂ“442. CLAUDE.md В§3 now retains the newest three: #135 OWNER-PROVEN (2026-07-17), the inventory reconcile/confidence recon (2026-07-17, ledger #137), ledger #136 (owner-test board + OP-14). Entries in == entries out: 3 in В§3 + 1 new = 4 в†’ 1 archived + 3 kept. Archive 182 в†’ 183. -->

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER THE FOUR WAYS A WORKING VARIETY GOT BROKEN THROUGH THE UI (ledger #135): D-49's invariant was right; three other mint paths didn't obey it and D-49's own create branch left half of it to a sheet that didn't argue вЂ” all four minted LIVE, through the UI, in the hour after D-49 was owner-proven; ledger #74's CASE 5 is now CONFIRMED-LIVE, not theoretical; BUILDER-COMPLETE, owner-proof owed; ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12); SHA `313de44`

**Type:** App code вЂ” 4 NEW (`packages/shared/src/components/FieldError.tsx` the FIX 5 pattern extracted В· `packages/shared/src/discovery/dupSize.test.ts` 22 В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/components/datasheet/flagCounts.ts` + `.test.ts` 12) + 8 EDIT (`shared/inventory/variantGroup.ts` `baseSkuOf` rehomed + `suggestSiblingSku` В· `shared/inventory/countPromote.ts` the `refuse` action В· `shared/inventory/countPromote.test.ts` 52 В· `shared/inventory/index.ts` В· `shared/inventory/stockLineResolver.ts` miss carries candidates В· `shared/discovery/dupSize.ts` `sizeGroupKey` + `findSizeTwin` В· `shared/pages/Settings.tsx` + `cultivar/pages/Discounts.tsx` repointed onto the shared FIX 5 В· `cultivar/components/inventory/InventoryEditor.tsx` В· `cultivar/pages/InventoryCount.tsx` В· `cultivar/pages/BusinessInventory.tsx` В· `cultivar/components/datasheet/DataSheet.tsx`) + 1 BANNER-MARKED STALE (`docs/decisions/2026-07-16-d49-stub-fold-remediation.sql`). **NO migration** (every column exists вЂ” these are validation and derivation fixes; none was needed, none taken). **ZERO new `api/` file** (12/12, counted). `[TRACE:INVENTORY]`/`[TRACE:COUNT]`/`[TRACE:RESOLVE]`/`[TRACE:invsheet]` ON вЂ” **net +2 emits, none silenced**. `npm run verify` **exit 0 zero NET-NEW** (tsc 5 / eslint 248 / knip 10В·14В·15 == baseline); `build:cultivar` clean 2353 modules 6.33s. **Full suite 16/16 green, zero regressions.** Ledger row #135. **NO new decision** вЂ” implements D-49 + D-46 + STD-011.

**THE INVARIANT вЂ” ONE statement, FOUR instances (this is why it is one build and not four):** *any path that mints or fills a row in a variety family leaves that family PICKER-READY BY CONSTRUCTION вЂ” every row grouped, every size non-empty and distinct, SKU derived from the family's BASE.* D-49 proved it on the count path and **the count path is now the reference implementation**. Three other paths did not obey it. **Every one of these four was minted live, through the UI, in the hour after D-49 was owner-proven** вЂ” not theorized.

**DEFECT 1 вЂ” SIZE REQUIRED (рџ”ґ the one that broke a clean variety with no warning).** David counted Alley Cat, left the size box EMPTY, entered 60, and it **SAVED**: `promote вЂ” created {variant_group:'alley-cat-redbud-espalier', size: null, qty: 60}` beside 15/30/45 в†’ re-scan **UNKNOWN**. **Clean at 16:59, broken at 17:03, by the branch that was supposed to be the fix.** The sheet said "Pick an existing size or type a new one", the field was optional, the save button did not argue. FIX: the refusal lives in **`resolveCountTarget`** and **the sheet ASKS THAT SAME FUNCTION** вЂ” so the UI gate and the write gate are ONE rule that cannot drift, rather than two rules that look alike until one changes (D-48's shape; В§1.6 item 3 вЂ” validated before the WRITE, not merely hidden in the UI). Refusal red-borders the size field and SAYS WHY (FIX 5); **never a greyed button with no reason**.

**вљ пёЏ IT REFUSES *BEFORE* THE EXACT-MATCH BRANCH вЂ” and that ordering IS the fix, plus the ugliest finding of the session.** A blank size **MATCHES** a size-less row (`sameSizeLabel(null,null)` is true), so a **stub counted with no size took a plain `update`**: qty landed on a row that still had no size, turning a harmless placeholder into a **size-LESS LOT** вЂ” the Basham's shape, minted one branch over. **D-49's own test suite asserted that as CORRECT** (`'stub counted with NO size в†’ plain update'`). **The defect was tested IN and blessed вЂ” the D-48 scar, verbatim, in the suite written to prevent this exact family of bug.** Refusing after (0) would have fixed `create` and left `update` live. Corrected, and flagged as a correction вЂ” **not a test adjusted to make a fix pass** (the 36 that passed in RED stayed green throughout).

**DEFECT 2 вЂ” DUP-SIZE GUARD (mints CASE 5).** Live and **still in the data**: `DISC-1002` and `DISC-1002-15G`, **same group, same size**. That is **ledger #73/#74's *same-group-same-size* dead end, carried as THEORETICAL since 2026-06-30 вЂ” minted through the UI in under a minute.** **It is now CONFIRMED-LIVE.** ROOT: D-46's editor enforces **SKU** uniqueness (`DISC-1002-15G` is unique в†’ guard passed) and never checked **SIZE** uniqueness within the group. **Two different facts, only one guarded** вЂ” a SKU identifies one sellable UNIT, a `(variant_group, size)` pair identifies one VARIANT; WooCommerce and Shopify both refuse a duplicate option combination *independently* of SKU (В§6 r16). FIX: new shared **`findSizeTwin`** sits **beside** `findDuplicateSizeGroups` on **ONE key** вЂ” same fact, opposite moment (the detector surfaces a collision that exists; the guard refuses to mint one), so the two can never disagree about what "the same size in the same group" means. It **names the twin and its SKU and offers to edit that row** вЂ” never a bare refusal.

**DEFECT 3 вЂ” SKU BASE (lineage compounded).** Live: **`DISC-1003-30G-45G`**. "+ Add size" from the `DISC-1003-30G` row handed **that** row's SKU to `deriveSiblingSku`; the next would have been `DISC-1003-30G-45G-15G`. **THE HELPER IS NOT THE BUG вЂ” THE CALLER IS**, and the proof is in David's own trail: the **count path called the SAME helper minutes later, on this SAME family** (which already contained the compounded SKU) and produced **`DISC-1003-60G` correctly** вЂ” because it resolved the base first. **Base resolution worked in one place and the other place didn't use it.** RECON ANSWER: the rule **was already a named shared function** вЂ” `baseSkuOf` (shortest non-blank SKU; a derived sibling is always base+suffix, so the base is always shortest) вЂ” but it was homed in `countPromote.ts`, which the editor has no business importing. **MOVED to `variantGroup.ts` beside `deriveSiblingSku` (SKU lineage is a SKU fact, not a counting fact)**; both minting paths now make the identical **`suggestSiblingSku(family, size)`** call. `deriveSiblingSku`'s idempotence guard only ever caught re-appending the *same* suffix вЂ” documented at the helper, since it cannot detect a wrong base.

**DEFECT 4 вЂ” BANNER SCOPED TO THE VIEW (Surface Honesty inverted).** Filtered to "alley": **4 clean rows, zero collisions**, and a red banner reading *"2 size collisions вЂ¦ Edit the size or variant group on a flagged row to fix it."* That was **Acoma's** collision, elsewhere, **rendered over four clean rows, instructing him to edit a flagged row when none was on screen**. **D-9 INVERTED: it does not fabricate a value, it MIS-ATTRIBUTES a real one** вЂ” the number was true, the place was a lie. FIX: `inView` and `elsewhere` are now **two separate honest facts**; the copy never lets one masquerade as the other, and a filtered-clean screen gets *"N flagged rows **elsewhere** вЂ¦ nothing on this screen is affected."* **The row FLAG stays catalog-wide on purpose** вЂ” a collision is a fact about the data, so a row must not stop being flagged because its twin scrolled out of view; only the COUNT is view-scoped, and **that distinction is the whole fix**. FOLD-IN: the copy said "2 collisions" while the trace said `Array(1)` вЂ” **ONE collision, TWO rows**; copy and trace now count the **same noun** and both are named. The rule was extracted **pure** (`flagCounts.ts`) because it was **unreachable inside a `useMemo`** вЂ” the D-49 lesson, one layer up.

**RECON CHANGED THE BUILD IN THREE PLACES (stated, because two of them contradict the prompt's own lean вЂ” as it invited):**
- **(a) The UNKNOWN sheet is NOT "a different surface with no family to break."** It runs **resolve-before-create**, so a typed name can land **INSIDE an existing family**; and on a genuinely new variety it **always** sets `groupKey = slugify(name)` вЂ” **every row it mints is in a family by construction**. Same family, same blast radius. Size is required there too, and `Size (optional)` в†’ `Size *`. *"Skip & flag"* (record-only, no promote) correctly needs no size вЂ” nothing is written for a size to describe.
- **(b) вљ пёЏ THE PROMPT'S QUOTED TRACE IS NOT WHAT THE CODE EMITS.** `(ungrouped siblings)` is a **hardcoded literal** that fires on *every* ambiguous miss. **Alley Cat's four rows were ALL grouped** вЂ” the cause was a blank size вЂ” so the emit **named the wrong cause, confidently**, and *"blank size on 1 row"* was a **human inference, not the trace's output**. So `[TRACE:RESOLVE]` told **two** lies, not one: the false `MISS` after `AMBIGUOUS`, **and** a fabricated cause. FIX: **ONE emit per miss**, which **SHOWS the candidates** `{size, group, sku}` rather than asserting a cause вЂ” the blank size becomes *visible* **without re-spelling `detectSizeCollision`'s predicate anywhere** (a second copy of that rule, written only to explain the first, is exactly what drifts вЂ” STD-011). The resolver's ambiguous miss now carries `candidates` (additive).
- **(c) The size-required rule is NOT unconditional in the EDITOR, and that is the invariant stated exactly, not a convenience conditional.** A row **in a variety group** must have a non-empty distinct size; a row with **no group** is a standalone item (netting, a tool) and legitimately needs none. "+ Add size" always joins a family, so it is always covered. On the COUNT path it *is* effectively unconditional, because the count always assigns a group.

**STD-002 вЂ” RED FIRST, ALL THREE ARTIFACTS, AGAINST THE REAL DEFECTS.** Following D-49's proven shape, the **CURRENT SHIPPED RULES WERE PORTED VERBATIM INTO THE NEW PURE MODULES FIRST** so the failures were against the real thing and not a strawman: `suggestSiblingSku` seeded from the clicked row В· `findSizeTwin` returned null always (the editor's actual behavior: no size guard at all) В· `partitionFlagged` counted over ALL rows. **RED: countPromote 36/16-fail В· dupSize 16/6-fail В· flagCounts 6/6-fail в†’ GREEN: 52/0 В· 22/0 В· 12/0.** Full suite **16/16, zero regressions** (canonicalName 34/34, catalog-variants 31/31, customerIdentity 22/22, tierPricing 138/138, populate 9/9).

**вљ пёЏ ONE OF MY OWN RED CHECKS FAILED LEGITIMATELY вЂ” and it found a real defect I hadn't been sent for.** I asserted the dup key could not be forged; it could. `sizeGroupKey('a||b','c')` and `sizeGroupKey('a','b||c')` **both** produced `a||b||c`, while the code carried the comment *"'||' separator в†’ no cross-pair key collision"* вЂ” **false since #74**, and `variant_group` is **owner-editable free text on the grid**, so it is reachable, not hypothetical. Consequence is mild (a false collision FLAG, never a wrong write) but **a key that can be forged is not a key**. Now a JSON pair вЂ” **the comment describes what the code does**.

**`detectSizeCollision` NOT TOUCHED.** It refused to guess on a blank size and on a mixed group and was **RIGHT both times** вЂ” that refusal is D-9 working and **it is what surfaced all of this**. We fixed the paths that CREATE the states it refuses; we did not loosen the refusal.

**В§6 r8 CONSOLIDATION (reported per the rule):** the **FIX 5 pattern extracted to ONE home** (`shared/components/FieldError.tsx`). The rule of three was **already exceeded**: `errBorder` in **3 byte-identical copies**, `FieldError` in **2**, plus **4 hand-inlined copies** of its `<p>` body вЂ” and **Discounts' own comment pointed at Settings as "the reference" while holding a copy** (the copies knew they were copies). All four consumers repointed. Also: `BusinessInventory`'s local `dupKey` retired onto `sizeGroupKey`; `InventoryCount`'s `existingSkus` prop replaced by ONE `peers` prop from which the editor derives **both** guards вЂ” **a caller can no longer supply one guard's data and forget the other's, which is precisely how the size guard came to be missing while the SKU guard shipped**.

**CONSTRAINTS HELD:** STD-011 (ONE key `sizeGroupKey` В· ONE base rule `baseSkuOf` В· ONE dup fact, two moments В· ONE validation pattern В· ONE size rule serving create AND per-field edit) В· STD-002 (REDв†’GREEN, all three) В· STD-017 (all 7 surfaces enumerated; **the EDIT path is a surface and got the same guard** вЂ” blanking a grouped row's size, or editing a size onto its twin, breaks the family exactly as create does) В· STD-018 В· D-9 (no fabricated SKU/base; omit-not-fake) В· D-34 (the lot IS the SKU; size is the distinguishing attribute) В· AC-1 (size stays a free-text VALUE вЂ” no CHECK, no enum) В· AC-3 (all reads/writes `business_id`-scoped) В· В§6 r11 (12/12, counted, none minted) В· В§6 r16 (the standard named: a variant is identified by its option-value combination; every commerce platform refuses an empty required option value and a duplicate combination). **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10).

**DEPLOYED-BAR SIGNAL (В§9 вЂ” committed в‰  live):** the signal **ONLY this build emits** is **a blank-size save being BLOCKED with a red-bordered size field and a visible reason**, and `[TRACE:INVENTORY] promote вЂ” REFUSED at the sheet: size-required`. **If a blank-size save still SUCCEEDS, you are testing OLD code вЂ” hard-refresh, confirm the Vercel deploy is READY and the bundle hash changed, and do NOT declare pass/fail** (the #128/#129 stale-cached-bundle scar).

**OWNER-PROVEN owed (David, live):** **(1)** Count a variety, leave size **BLANK** в†’ **BLOCKED with a visible reason**, no row written, re-scan still resolves. **(2)** Count a variety, type a **NEW** size в†’ creates, SKU `DISC-####-NNG` **from the PARENT base**, re-scan в†’ picker fires with all sizes. **(3)** "+ Add size" with a size **already in the group** в†’ **BLOCKED, names that row and offers to edit it**. **(4)** "+ Add size" from a **SUFFIXED sibling** (`DISC-1003-30G`) в†’ mints `DISC-1003-NNG`, **NOT** `DISC-1003-30G-NNG`. **(5)** Filter `/inventory` to a clean variety в†’ **no "collisions here" banner** (an *"elsewhere"* note is correct and expected while Acoma's twin is still in the data); filter to **Acoma** в†’ banner fires and names the right rows. **(6)** An **AMBIGUOUS** resolve does **NOT** also emit a false `MISS` вЂ” one emit, listing the candidates. `[TRACE:*]` stays ON until owner-proven.

**вљ пёЏ THE #133 FOLD SQL IS STALE вЂ” DO NOT APPLY IT, AND DO NOT ASK DAVID TO.** It was written against **118** rows; David's catalog is at **123** and its row count **moved eight times** during the owner-prove. Its scope has also **GROWN**: the remediation now owes the four orphan pairs **PLUS Acoma's CASE-5 twin PLUS `DISC-1003-30G-45G`**. **It gets REGENERATED against a SETTLED catalog AFTER these fixes are owner-proven** вЂ” applying a hard-coded-id DELETE against a moving catalog is exactly the scar its own guards were built to avoid. I **banner-marked the file itself в›” STALE / DO NOT APPLY** rather than only writing it here: a file that says "apply me" when it must not be applied is the same dead-affordance class this entire build is about. Its **G1-G5 guard structure and its ORDERING lesson** (repoint `inventory_counts` BEFORE the fold вЂ” the FK is `ON DELETE SET NULL`, so deleting first silently orphans the count rather than erroring) are the **template** the regenerated version is built from. **The 103 stubs and the ungrouped rows get NO SQL** вЂ” they are not broken; the code fix makes their first count correct.

**вљ пёЏ OP-13's FIRST LIVE TEST вЂ” IT PASSED, AND IT EXPOSED ITS OWN LIMIT. Measured, not computed (I do not get to make this claim by arithmetic вЂ” the D-49 lesson bit that entry twice).** The gate **fired at step 0 without a prompt reminding it to**, which is the only property that matters for a rule that must survive being forgotten: 3 in В§3 + 1 new = 4 в†’ **1 archived (`e6211927вЂ¦` in and out, byte-identical) + 3 kept**; archive **179 в†’ 180**; line 3 still a pointer. **BUT: CLAUDE.md went 736 в†’ 753 lines / 118,939 chars вЂ” it GREW, on the session that archived an entry.** Per-section, measured: **В§3 91 в†’ 104 (+13)** *despite* losing an entry, because **N=3 caps the COUNT of entries and nothing caps their SIZE** вЂ” mine is simply longer than the one it displaced. And the **Tech Debt Log grew 1,468 characters while adding ~0 LINES** (it is one physical line). **That is the line-3 finding happening AGAIN, in the very write-back that proposes fixing it** вЂ” the metric is blind to exactly the growth that is occurring. So: **the retention rule works and is not sufficient**; the honest reading is that OP-13 bounds the handoff's *shape*, and the remaining growth is prose in В§-blocks the line count cannot see. **This is the concrete case for the char-budget amendment (d) below, and it is evidence, not an argument.**

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built):** **(a) tech-debt #56 вЂ” size VOCABULARY**, the next defect in this family and now the *only* live one: `sameSizeLabel`/`findSizeTwin` are exact string equality and the catalog carries **six spellings of three sizes** (`15`,`30`,`45`,`5 gal`,`30 gal`,`45 gal`), including on **'Sierra', a DEMO variety**. Counting "15 gal" against a "15" chip mints a THIRD row. D-45 did resolve-before-create for NAMES; nobody did it for SIZES. **Its own build вЂ” unlike these four it can MERGE existing rows, so it needs a read-only blast-radius probe first.** **(b) tech-debt #58 (NEW) вЂ” the DB-level guard**: the durable form of defect 2 is a partial unique index `(business_id, variant_group, size) WHERE both NOT NULL`. That is ledger #74's deferred option C. **It would REJECT the live Acoma dup, so it cannot land until the data is clean.** Sibling of **#54** (the `qb_customer_id` partial unique index вЂ” named, not taken, same reasoning): the code guard is proportionate at a single-owner nursery's volume. **(c) tech-debt #59 (NEW) вЂ” `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md`'s header carries the STD-011 duplicate-header disease AND IS LOADED EVERY SESSION** (В§10 Session Starter opens it FIRST). **My own OP-13 triage put it with the ledger and DECISIONS-INDEX as "not loaded every session" вЂ” that was WRONG for this one**, and it is a per-session token tax exactly like line 3 was. The ledger/DECISIONS-INDEX triage stands. **(d) THE ~600-LINE BUDGET MEASURES THE WRONG QUANTITY** вЂ” my own OP-13 finding proved it: line 3 was **ONE line and ~1,400 tokens**. Lines are a bad proxy the moment prose enters the file. **Proposed OP-13 amendment: switch the budget to CHARACTERS (`wc -c` вЂ” as cheap as `wc -l`, doesn't lie about prose). David rules.** **(e)** `findDuplicateSizeGroups` is **still blind to blank-size rows** ([dupSize.ts:44](packages/shared/src/discovery/dupSize.ts#L44)) вЂ” reported, **deliberately not widened**; asserted as a KNOWN BLIND SPOT in the new suite so it is visible rather than assumed handled. Widening it would make a landmine visible on the grid BEFORE it blows. **(f)** The count's `create` branch **regroups blank-group rows including a blank-SIZE one**, which would put an unpickable row *into* a group вЂ” **unreachable today** (a stub is always alone in its family, and post-fix nothing can mint a size-less row), so **deliberately NOT guarded** per В§6 r10's over-engineering trap. If #56's merge work touches this, revisit. **(g)** A **legacy size-less row with qty>0** (Alley Cat's, which David deleted by hand) can no longer be created but also cannot be *healed* by the count вЂ” it would be refused. **No live instance exists**; the regenerated remediation is where it belongs.

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER В§3 HANDOFF RETENTION (N=3): the trim PLUS the rule that makes it self-maintaining вЂ” the close-out protocol was manufacturing bloat faster than any trim removed it; OP-13 (proposed); 907 в†’ 736 lines, 12 entries moved VERBATIM, ZERO lost; DOCS-ONLY вЂ” zero code/schema/migration/api-fn (12/12)

**Type:** Docs/process only вЂ” 5 files (`docs/operating-doctrine/end-of-session-protocol.md` NEW gate В· `CLAUDE.md` line-3 header + В§9 standing instruction + close-step 0 + В§3 trim + В§4 tick В· `docs/handoff-archive.md` +12 entries В· `docs/DECISIONS.md` OP-13 В· `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` OP row + drift line) + ledger #134 + bootstrap CAPTURE INDEX row. **ZERO app code, ZERO schema, ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12 untouched), ZERO `[TRACE:*]`** вЂ” no `.ts`/`.tsx` opened. `npm run verify` NOT run (no code changed; the gate is code-scoped). Decision **OP-13 (proposed)** вЂ” number **CONFIRMED, not guessed**: OP-1вЂ¦OP-12 exist in `DECISIONS.md` headings AND the `DECISIONS-INDEX` table, so OP-13 is next. **BENCH: no trigger fires** (no payments/PII/webhooks/AI-calls/invoices/compliance-actions вЂ” roster matched, reported per В§6 r10).

**THE PROBLEM вЂ” measured, not asserted.** CLAUDE.md was trimmed to 746 lines and measured **907** today: **+100 in one session**, and **+44 more on a build whose write-back was deliberately kept minimal** (that session resisted inventing a В§4 task and it grew anyway). **Trimming to 600 buys about five sessions.** The close-out protocol вЂ” six standing instructions each demanding a write-back вЂ” manufactures the bloat faster than any trim removes it. **So a trim is a payment against a recurring cost, and the deliverable is NOT a trim: it is a trim PLUS a retention rule.** This is **AC-4** applied to the handoff (settle once, encode as a variable, stop re-deciding every session) and a partial delivery of В§4 Housekeeping's existing unchecked *"Lean CLAUDE.md to rules + state + pointers only."*

**THE RULE (written BEFORE the trim, so the trim is its FIRST application, not a one-off):** **В§3 holds the most recent THREE entries. At every close-out, BEFORE the new entry is written, any entry beyond the newest three is MOVED вЂ” verbatim, not summarized вЂ” to `docs/handoff-archive.md`, newest-first, under a dated provenance comment.** Nothing deleted, nothing condensed; append-and-preserve. **A close-out that writes a В§3 entry without archiving the overflow is an INCOMPLETE task вЂ” same force as the built-inventory, close-out-ledger, and вљЎ ACTIVE STATUS gates.** Homed exactly as those siblings are: statement of force = `docs/operating-doctrine/end-of-session-protocol.md` в†’ **GATE вЂ” CLAUDE.md В§3 HANDOFF RETENTION** (matching the BUILT-INVENTORY gate's shape); pointer = a В§9 STANDING INSTRUCTION; execution = **close-sequence step 0**.

**вљ пёЏ STEP 0, NOT STEP 1 вЂ” a deliberate call, stated.** The rule fires BEFORE the handoff write, so it is numbered **0**. Inserting it as "1" would have renumbered steps 1вЂ“11, and **В§1.6 item 9 cites "В§9 gate 9"** while steps 10/11 cite "item 8"/"item 9" вЂ” renumbering would have silently broken four live cross-references to fix a formatting preference. Step 0 is both semantically correct and cross-reference-safe.

**KILLED THE DUPLICATE HEADER (STD-011 вЂ” and the finding the line-count metric HIDES).** Line 3's `# Last updated:` was a **~600-word prose block restating the newest В§3 entry verbatim** вЂ” a SECOND representation of one fact, exactly the disease STD-011 names. **It is ONE physical line, so removing it saves ~1 line but ~1,400 tokens on every session load.** That is the honest catch: **line count is a PROXY, and the single worst offender was invisible to it.** Replaced with a one-line pointer + a standing warning line (`вљ пёЏ THIS LINE IS A POINTER, NEVER A SUMMARY`) so it cannot regrow вЂ” the rule's second clause.

**APPLIED вЂ” NOTHING LOST, PROVEN BY SHA NOT BY ASSERTION.** 12 entries (D-48 `2026-07-16` в†’ D-39 `2026-07-13`) moved to the archive. **Verified: the 201-line block extracted from CLAUDE.md hashed `564f09d6вЂ¦`; the block removed hashed `564f09d6вЂ¦`; `diff` = empty в‡’ byte-identical.** Entry arithmetic: **14 in В§3 в†’ 12 archived + 2 kept + 1 new = 3 in В§3; 14 preserved, 0 lost.** Archive **167 в†’ 179** entries (7,501 в†’ 7,704 lines), newest-first, chronologically continuous (its newest was `2026-07-10`; В§3's oldest was `2026-07-13` вЂ” **no gap, no overlap**). В§3 now holds: this entry В· **D-49** В· **#55 apostrophe elide**.

**вљ пёЏ THE RESULT, REPORTED HONESTLY вЂ” N=3 DOES NOT HIT 600.** **907 в†’ 736 lines (в€’171). That is 136 OVER the ~600 budget, and it is a MISS.** N=3 was David's call and **no N was improvised to hit the number** вЂ” tuning the variable to flatter the metric would defeat the point of encoding it. **(вљ пёЏ Own scar, same session, TWICE: this entry first stated **711** from arithmetic в†’ the file measured **732**; corrected to 732 в†’ the file then measured **736** (the В§4 edit landed after the read). **Two consecutive predicted counts, both wrong.** The D-49 lesson вЂ” *"trust the catalog, never the arithmetic written down"* вЂ” bit the very entry that cites it, and bit it again on the correction. The rule is not "compute carefully," it is **measure last and quote the measurement**; every figure here is now `wc -l` output taken after the final edit.)** The residual is **structural, not handoff**: В§3 is now **91 lines of 736**; the weight is В§2's infra tables (~155), В§6's ten coding rules (~45), В§9's standing instructions (~55). **This gate stops the GROWTH; it does not by itself close the budget** вЂ” the rest is the separate, still-open В§4 item *"Lean CLAUDE.md to rules + state + pointers only,"* which stays open and un-ticked apart from the one sub-item this closes. **The self-maintaining property is the win, not the number:** В§3 can no longer grow past 3 entries, and the header can no longer regrow into a summary.

**CONSTRAINTS HELD:** **STD-005** (decisions recorded verbatim, not paraphrased вЂ” this is why the move is a byte-identical `diff`, not a re-write; and the stale В§3 footer blockquote, which still described the retired "retains only the most-recent date-block" practice, was REPLACED cleanly rather than left contradicting the new rule) В· **STD-011** (the header duplicate killed; one fact, one home) В· **AC-4** (settle once, encode as N=3) В· **В§6 r10** (standard-by-value: a retention-N is the established doc-rotation pattern; adopted at the cheapest form that meets the need вЂ” no tooling, no automation, no lint rule, because the gate rides the close-out ritual that already exists) В· **В§1.6** вЂ” most items **N/A and stated as such** (2 hardcoded-register / 3 validation / 4 CRUD-permissions / 5 UI-modals / 6 AC-1..4 / 7 12-fn / 10 money-safety all N/A: no code, no schema, no UI, no endpoint, no money path); **applicable:** 1 STORY (docs/process infra вЂ” no user story required, same class as ledger #38/#46/#50, stated at STEP 0), 8 REUSE (the gate reuses the existing BUILT-INVENTORY gate shape + the В§9 standing-instruction pattern вЂ” no new mechanism invented), 9 TRACE-STAYS-ON (nothing silenced; no `[TRACE:*]` touched).

**NO OWNER-PROVE OWED вЂ” and the reason is the point.** This is process scaffold, not a capability: there is no UI, no RLS path, no live behavior to exercise. **It proves itself on the NEXT close-out** вЂ” if the next session archives its overflow and leaves line 3 a pointer, the rule works; if В§3 shows a 4th entry, the gate failed and that is visible at a glance. **The gate is its own test.**

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built вЂ” all deliberately out of this bounded pass):** **(a) рџ”ґ `GEMINI.md` DOES NOT EXIST** вЂ” CLAUDE.md's line-4/header instruction *"Update GEMINI.md with the same changes if Gemini is in use"* points at a **dead file**. Not silently synced, not silently deleted; David decides whether to retire the instruction. **(b) THE SAME DISEASE IS IN THREE MORE HEADERS** вЂ” `docs/CLOSE-OUT-LEDGER.md`, `TRACE-SESSION-BOOTSTRAP.md`, and `docs/DECISIONS-INDEX.md` each open with a giant `Last updated:` prose block restating their newest row (the ledger's is ~400 words). **Same STD-011 violation, same token cost, same fix** вЂ” but they are NOT loaded every session, so the cost is far lower and it was out of scope here. The header-is-a-pointer clause could extend to them on David's call. **(c) OP-11 has no CAPTURE INDEX row** (OP-1/4/5/6/7/8/9/10/12 do; OP-2/3/11 don't) вЂ” noticed while adding OP-13's row, left alone as scope. **(d)** The В§4 Doc Reorg block's other three items (PLATFORM_STRATEGY as sole architecture home В· BUILT-INVENTORY pointers-not-inlines В· single-source the philosophy block) remain **un-ticked and untouched** вЂ” the reorg is a separate build and stays there.

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER D-49 THE COUNT FILLS THE STUB (tech-debt #57): counting a variety made it PERMANENTLY UNSCANNABLE вЂ” the #55 fix made a D-45 branch reachable that had never fired; ONE invariant, TWO branches; 103 of 112 scraped rows were landmines; BUILDER-COMPLETE, owner-proof owed; ZERO schema migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12), ONE gated data SQL

**Type:** App code вЂ” 2 NEW (`packages/shared/src/inventory/countPromote.ts` the PURE promote decision В· `.../countPromote.test.ts` 40 assertions) + 2 EDIT (`packages/shared/src/inventory/index.ts` barrel В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/InventoryCount.tsx` repointed onto the decision + `filled`/`created` trail + SKU lineage + uniqueness guard) + 1 GATED SQL (`docs/decisions/2026-07-16-d49-stub-fold-remediation.sql` вЂ” data remediation, **NOT a schema migration**, David applies as postgres). **NO schema change** (size/variant_group/sku all exist вЂ” confirmed, none needed), **ZERO new `api/` file** (12/12, counted). `[TRACE:INVENTORY]`/`[TRACE:COUNT]`/`[TRACE:RESOLVE]` ON (extended, nothing silenced). `npm run verify` **exit 0 zero NET-NEW** (tsc 5 / eslint 248 / knip 10В·14В·15 == baseline); `build:cultivar` clean 7.34s. Tests: **countPromote 40/40** (new) В· **canonicalName 34/34** (#55 unregressed) В· **catalog-variants 31/31** В· **populate 9/9**. Decision **D-49 (proposed)** (`docs/decisions/2026-07-16-count-fills-the-stub-D49.md`). Ledger row #133. **CLOSES tech-debt #57; RECORDS #56.**

**THE DEFECT (live, David's own trail вЂ” do NOT re-derive):** `[TRACE:RESOLVE] L4 name-token вЂ” bashams-party-pink-crape-myrtle в†’ Basham's Party Pink Crape Myrtle ... siblings: 1` immediately followed by `[TRACE:INVENTORY] promote вЂ” created {size: '30 gal', qty: 25, rowId: e92aedb2-вЂ¦}`. **It resolved to the existing row and then created a second one.** Inventory **114 в†’ 118** on four scans. The RE-SCAN then returned **UNKNOWN** вЂ” two token-equal rows, one grouped + one not, one sized + one not в†’ `detectSizeCollision` correctly refuses to guess. **NET: counting a variety makes it permanently unscannable**, self-inflicting, once per variety, on the walk-and-count loop the whole capability exists for.

**WHY IT FIRED NOW (the lesson):** **#55 made this branch REACHABLE for the first time.** Before the apostrophe elide those four varieties never resolved at L4 at all вЂ” they went straight to UNKNOWN and never reached the promote. **Fixing the resolver handed a working scan to a promote that had never been exercised on a size-less parent. A fix that unblocks a path is a fix that exposes that path's first real test.**

**THE ROOT:** `populate.ts` mints the scraped catalog under the D-9 honesty contract вЂ” `qty: 0` (never fabricate stock), `unit_cost: null`, and **no size at all** вЂ” because scrape-reads-variations was never built. **MEASURED LIVE (David, read-only): 119 rows В· 103 stubs В· 112 DISC- В· 103 DISC- stubs В· 5 ungrouped-sized. 108 of 119 rows would break on first count.** D-45's `(variety Г— size)` predicate never anticipated a size-**less** parent because nothing could resolve to one.

**WHY NOTHING CAUGHT IT:** the suite was green (catalog-variants 31/31, populate 9/9). **The promote decision lived inline in a React component's async handler вЂ” unreachable by any test.** And the grid's own dup detector `findDuplicateSizeGroups` is **BLIND to this class**: it skips any row with a blank size ([dupSize.ts:44](packages/shared/src/discovery/dupSize.ts#L44)), and the parent's size is blank вЂ” so the one surface built to surface this damage could not see it.

**THE FIX вЂ” ONE INVARIANT, STATED ONCE, GOVERNING BOTH BRANCHES:** *any path that mints a size-sibling must leave the family in a state where the size-picker fires **BY CONSTRUCTION** вЂ” a non-blank `variant_group` on EVERY row, a distinct non-empty size on EVERY row, and SKU lineage from the family's base.* That is exactly `detectSizeCollision`'s contract; violating it is not cosmetic вЂ” it is the UNKNOWN.
- **(1) STUB в†’ FILL IN PLACE** (qty 0 + no size + no group). **D-49 ENFORCES D-34, it does not bend it:** the lot IS the SKU and qty is the COUNT, so a row with no size AND no stock AND no size-family **cannot be a lot** вЂ” there is nothing for it to be the lot OF. It is a variety list wearing a stock-line's clothes. If the stub cannot be a lot, the first real lot must BECOME it. The filled row keeps its `DISC-####` SKU + scrape lineage.
- **(2) UNGROUPED NON-STUB в†’ CREATE **AND AUTO-GROUP THE PARENT** in the same pass** (5 live rows, e.g. Flip Side Vitex DISC-1104 qty 10 size 45 group NULL). **This is D-46's own rule (#126 вЂ” "auto-groups the parent if its group was null so the size-picker fires by construction") finally reaching the SECOND path that mints siblings** вЂ” `commitCount` backfilled `variant_group` on the **MATCH branch only**. Not a new rule; a convention that existed on one path and not the other (STD-011) вЂ” **the same disease as D-49's own root**.

**CREATE IS NOT THE DEFECT вЂ” CREATE-WHILE-LEAVING-THE-PARENT-UNGROUPED IS.** Proven by live data, not reasoning: `'Sierra' Mexican Red Oak` (groups BOTH set, sizes `["15","30 gal"]`, skus `[DISC-1001, NULL]`) and `Arizona Cypress Blue Ice` (`["15","30"]`, `[DISC-1005, NULL]`) were **minted by this SAME count-promote CREATE** вЂ” their siblings' `sku NULL` is that path's fingerprint вЂ” and **BOTH WORK**, because their parents already carried a group.

**STD-002 вЂ” RED FIRST, BOTH ARTIFACTS, HONEST:** to get a RED against the REAL defect (not a strawman) the CURRENT shipped logic was first ported verbatim into the new pure module в†’ **`countPromote: 24 passed, 16 failed` (exit 1)**, every one of the four varieties failing `RE-SCAN RESOLVES`. Then the fix в†’ **`40 passed, 0 failed` (exit 0)**. **No existing assertion was adjusted** вЂ” the 24 that passed in RED stayed green throughout. **The `'Sierra' UNREGRESSED` control PASSES EVEN IN RED** вЂ” it is a real control, not a rubber stamp.

**`detectSizeCollision` NOT TOUCHED** вЂ” refusing to guess between two same-name rows is **correct** and is what stopped a bad merge. The fix stops MANUFACTURING the ambiguous pair; it does not loosen the gate that catches it. The re-scan assertions call the **REAL shipped `detectSizeCollision`** against the simulated post-write family (the #55 lesson: verify against the real function, never the test's own copy).

**SKU LINEAGE (STD-011 drift, fixed this pass):** D-46 built `deriveSiblingSku` (#127) but **`InventoryCount` never imported it** вЂ” two paths minted a sibling, one derived the SKU, which is why David's four counted rows have no SKU. Repointed onto the SHARED helper. New `baseSkuOf` = **shortest non-blank SKU** in the family (a derived sibling is always its base + a suffix, so the base is always shortest вЂ” reaches past a sku-null sibling: `'Sierra'`'s 3rd size derives `DISC-1001-45G`, not from the NULL). Blank when no sibling has one (D-9 вЂ” never fabricate a base). **Uniqueness: a colliding derived SKU is OMITTED + logged loudly, never blocks the count** вЂ” the SKU is a convenience, the count is the point, and refusing a save mid-lot-walk is disproportionate; never a silent duplicate.

**вљ пёЏ THE LIVE PROBE WAS BLOCKED вЂ” AND THAT WAS SURFACED, NOT GUESSED (STD-001).** `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY=""` is **empty in every local env file**; anon is correctly RLS-blocked on `business_inventory` (0 rows). **This is the #129 scar verbatim** ("the first diagnosis was WRONG вЂ” it trusted the stale live-schema-map because the service key pulled empty"). Thunder **refused to write the DELETE against unverified ids** and David ran the read-only query. **He was right to be asked: the prompt said 114в†’118, the catalog said 119в†’115** (a Vitex 45 gal added via D-46 after the prompt was written) вЂ” and the prompt's "~113 stubs" was an inference; **the measured number is 103**. Third consecutive time a prompt's row count was wrong (111в†’116 on #55). **Trust the catalog, never the arithmetic written down.**

**DATA REMEDIATION вЂ” ONE GATED SQL, 8 rows, 4 folds.** FOLD the child INTO the parent (the parent holds the SKU + the sell_price slot + the 2026-06-26 scrape lineage), do NOT delete the count. **ORDER IS LOAD-BEARING:** `inventory_counts.inventory_id` is **ON DELETE SET NULL** вЂ” delete the child first and the record of the count David just did silently loses its subject (no error, just an orphan). So: **REPOINT `inventory_counts` childв†’parent FIRST, then fold, then delete.** One transaction; **five guards (G1-G5)** abort the whole thing вЂ” wrong row count, ANY `order_items` reference (**proven zero, not assumed** вЂ” the FK is SET NULL, so a delete would silently blank a sales line rather than error), a parent that is no longer a stub, a child that is not the count-created sibling, a parent/child name mismatch. **Every value is COPIED FROM THE CHILD ROW, never retyped from a prompt.** `status` deliberately untouched (mirrors the code's `fill` branch exactly; a scrape-flagged `'review'` parent stays `'review'` вЂ” surfaced in the pre-flight, not silently "fixed"). **Expected 119 в†’ 115 вЂ” but PART 0c/2E read the count from the catalog; do not trust the arithmetic.** **PART 3 is FENCED + commented-out + INDEPENDENT:** the SKU backfill for the two REAL size-siblings (`'Sierra'` 30 gal в†’ `DISC-1001-30G`, `Arizona` 30 в†’ `DISC-1005-30G`) вЂ” **DO NOT FOLD THEM, they are genuine second sizes under grouped parents and they work today**; matched BY PREDICATE (their ids weren't in the live read). Apply or skip; PART 1 neither depends on it nor is depended on.

**SCOPE HELD вЂ” the 103 stubs + 5 ungrouped rows get NO SQL.** They are not broken; the code fix makes their first count correct. Remediating them would be writing to 108 rows to fix nothing.

**CONSTRAINTS HELD:** STD-011 (ONE stub predicate `isVarietyStub` вЂ” never re-spelled inline; ONE `deriveSiblingSku`; ONE `sameSizeLabel` вЂ” the local `sameSize` copy RETIRED) В· STD-002 (REDв†’GREEN artifacts) В· STD-017 (all 6 surfaces enumerated; the fix is in the ONE shared decision so all inherit) В· STD-018 В· D-9 (no fabricated SKU/price/base) В· AC-1 (stub-ness derived from DATA вЂ” no CHECK, no enum) В· AC-3 (tenant-scoped; the SQL guards on `business_id`) В· В§6 r8 В· В§6 r11 (12/12 api-fn, counted, none minted).

**OWNER-PROVEN owed (David, live):** **(0)** apply `2026-07-16-d49-stub-fold-remediation.sql` as postgres вЂ” PART 0 read-only FIRST, then PART 1, then PART 2 verify (AвЂ“E). **(1)** Count a STUB variety (any of the 103) в†’ **ONE row**, filled, **SKU intact**, `[TRACE:INVENTORY] promote вЂ” filled` in the trail. **(2) RE-SCAN the same slug в†’ RESOLVES** (the defect that motivated D-49 вЂ” it must NOT go UNKNOWN). **(3)** Add a SECOND size to it в†’ a real sibling, SKU `DISC-####-NNG`, **size-picker fires with both**. **(4)** Count a second size on an UNGROUPED-SIZED row (Flip Side Vitex DISC-1104) в†’ sibling created AND `promote вЂ” auto-grouped parent` in the trail в†’ re-scan fires the picker. **(5)** `'Sierra' Mexican Red Oak` **UNREGRESSED** вЂ” both sizes resolve, picker still fires. **(6)** Vitex control unchanged. `[TRACE:*]` stays ON until owner-proven. вљ пёЏ **DEPLOYED bar FIRST (В§9 вЂ” committed в‰  live):** the signal **ONLY this build emits is `[TRACE:INVENTORY] promote вЂ” filled`**. **If a stub count still logs `promote вЂ” created`, you are testing OLD code вЂ” hard-refresh, confirm the Vercel deploy is READY and the bundle hash changed, and do NOT declare pass/fail** (the #128/#129 stale-cached-bundle scar).

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built):** **(a)** **tech-debt #56 вЂ” SIZE VOCABULARY, the next defect in this family** (David's own catch): `sameSizeLabel` is exact string equality and the catalog **already** carries mixed vocabulary (`'Sierra'` live with `["15","30 gal"]`) в†’ counting "15 gal" against a "15" row mints a THIRD row, splitting on-hand across two spellings of one physical size. D-45 did this for NAMES; nobody did it for SIZES; `normalizeSize` exists in the scrape parser, not on the count path вЂ” **the same one-path-not-the-other disease as D-49's own root**. Its own build: unlike D-49 it can **MERGE** existing rows, so it needs a read-only blast-radius probe first. **(b)** `findDuplicateSizeGroups` is blind to the mixed-group/missing-size class вЂ” widening it would make a landmine visible on the grid BEFORE it blows. **(c)** `populate.ts` still mints size-less stubs; the durable fix is scrape-reads-variations (a WRITE path over the whole catalog вЂ” its own build). **(d) вљ пёЏ `.env.prod.local` points `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` at `ufsgqckbxdtwviqjjtos`** вЂ” the OLD Ignition project (В§7 **Off Limits**). Any local script run against that file targets the wrong project; it is inert **only because the project is now ENOTFOUND**. `.env.vercel.pulled` carries the correct `bgobkjcopcxusjsetfob`. **David decides** вЂ” but a В§7-off-limits project as a local default is a loaded gun. **(e) row-count discrepancy:** the #55 probe read **116** this morning; the app's `[TRACE:invsheet] load ok` reported **114** at the same point. A 2-row gap вЂ” likely `BusinessInventory` filtering a status the probe doesn't. **A probe and a page that disagree on row count will produce a false alarm on the next sweep.** **(f)** ledger #74's dup-size dead-end: **the DESTINATION is now CONFIRMED LIVE, but CASE 5 itself is not** вЂ” #74's case is *same group, same size* (fails the all-distinct check); this was *mixed group, missing size* (fails the group-shared + size-present checks). **Same dead end, different gate, different road.** #74 called it theoretical; it isn't вЂ” it just arrived by a road nobody was watching.

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER APOSTROPHE ELIDE in `nameTokenSet` (tech-debt #55): the possessive false-UNKNOWN вЂ” 4 of 6 apostrophe varieties were silently unscannable in LIVE data; the 1-char filter that kills the botanical hybrid `x` was eating the possessive `s`; implements D-45 (NO new decision); BUILDER-COMPLETE, owner-proof owed; ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12)

**Type:** App code вЂ” 3 files (`packages/shared/src/utils/canonicalName.ts` the fix В· `.../canonicalName.test.ts` +13 assertions В· `packages/cultivar-os/src/pages/InventoryCount.tsx` `[TRACE:RESOLVE]` token-set trail). **NO migration**, **ZERO new `api/` file** (12/12). `[TRACE:RESOLVE]`/`[TRACE:COUNT]` ON (extended, nothing silenced). `npm run verify` **exit 0 zero NET-NEW** (tsc 5 / eslint 248 / knip 10В·14В·15 == baseline); `build:cultivar` clean 2350 modules. Tests: **canonicalName 34/34** (was 21) В· **customerIdentity 22/22** (D-47 unregressed) В· **catalog-variants 31/31** (the `populate.ts` consumer). Ledger row #132. **CLOSES tech-debt #55.**

**THE DEFECT (live, not theorized):** `nameTokenSet` treated the apostrophe as a token BOUNDARY, and the 1-char filter вЂ” which exists to kill the botanical hybrid marker `x` вЂ” then ate the orphaned fragment: `"Basham's Party Pink Crape Myrtle"` в†’ `{basham,party,pink,crape,myrtle}` while its own WordPress slug `bashams-party-pink-crape-myrtle` в†’ `{bashams,вЂ¦}` в‡’ **NO MATCH в†’ false-UNKNOWN at scan**. `"Hearts A'fire Redbud"` в†’ `{hearts,fire}` вЂ” the `a` eaten outright. **Four of six apostrophe varieties in the live catalog were unscannable, all `variant_group` NULL, never counted.** This is precisely the class `canonicalName` exists to fix.

**WHY IT HID (the lesson):** **WRAPPING quotes survive either way** вЂ” `'Sierra'` в†’ `{sierra}`, because the quotes sit at word boundaries whether you split on them or elide them. The entire D-45/D-46 size-variant arc was owner-proven on **`'Sierra' Mexican Red Oak`**, which has no possessive. **The one case anybody eyeballs works.** The defect was also already WRITTEN DOWN вЂ” `personName.ts:27-30` flagged it in prose during D-47 ("a QR slug `baileys-red-twig-dogwood` will not match a catalog `Bailey's Red Twig Dogwood`") and correctly deferred it as owner-proven territory needing its own provable build. This is that build.

**THE FIX вЂ” ORDER OF OPERATIONS IS THE FIX:** ELIDE the apostrophe **BEFORE** the non-alnum boundary split. Split first and `A'fire` в†’ `a` + `fire` в†’ the 1-char filter eats the `a` в†’ `{fire}`. Verified explicitly and asserted (`nameTokenSet("Hearts A'fire").has('afire')`). **Codepoints handled: U+0027 `'` В· U+2019 `вЂ™` (what most CMSs emit) В· U+02BC `Кј` В· U+0060 `` ` `` В· U+00B4 `Вґ`** вЂ” live data carries only U+0027 today (probed), but the scrape source is WordPress, and a curly-apostrophe miss is the same bug wearing a different codepoint. **The 1-char filter STAYS** вЂ” its hybrid-`x` reason still holds; eliding merely stops feeding it orphans it was never meant to eat (asserted: `!nameTokenSet("Acer x 'Autumn Blaze'").has('x')`).

**В§6 r16 вЂ” THE STANDARD, STATED, WITH ITS ONE DELIBERATE DEVIATION:** the established pattern (Lucene ASCIIFoldingFilter + WordDelimiterGraphFilter; Postgres `unaccent`) is fold в†’ ELIDE intra-word punctuation в†’ split on true word boundaries в†’ drop stop-tokens. **The standard never splits on an apostrophe.** DEVIATION: Lucene **STRIPS** the English possessive (`Basham's` в†’ `basham`); we **ELIDE** it (`Basham's` в†’ `bashams`). REASON: our comparison target is a WordPress `sanitize_title` slug, which elides the apostrophe and emits `bashams-party-pink` вЂ” **stripping would yield `{basham}` and STILL miss.** Elide matches the source convention, and is the shape `personName.ts` already proved in D-47.

**STD-002 вЂ” RED FIRST, BOTH ARTIFACTS CAPTURED:** four failing assertions added BEFORE any source change в†’ **`canonicalName: 21 passed, 4 failed` (exit 1)**, each of the four named varieties failing. Then the fix в†’ **`canonicalName: 34 passed, 0 failed` (exit 0)**. **No existing assertion was adjusted to make the fix pass** вЂ” all 21 pre-existing stayed green throughout.

**вљ пёЏ BLAST RADIUS вЂ” PROBED LIVE, READ-ONLY, BEFORE TOUCHING SOURCE (STD-001).** `populate.ts` is a WRITE path (`canonicalNameKey(v.slug) === canonicalNameKey(item.variety)` decides which rows GROUP at import), so a changed key was the real risk. Probed against the **REAL catalog вЂ” 116 rows, NOT the 111 the prompt assumed** (flagged): **exactly 4 token sets change** (only the four broken varieties, no others) В· **L4 pairwise: 0 un-grouped, 0 re-grouped** В· **populate.ts import: 4 FIXED, 0 BROKEN** В· **0 groups merging distinct varieties**. **No currently-working grouping moves в‡’ no STOP condition.** The only changes are brokenв†’fixed, which is the build's intent. Re-verified against the **REAL shipped function** afterward (not just the probe's re-implementation).

**STD-017 вЂ” EVERY SURFACE ENUMERATED, all true (the fix is in the ONE shared fn, so they inherit):** (a) `InventoryCount.tsx` handleScan L4 (`stockLineResolver.ts:112-119`) вњ… В· (b) the typed no-QR resolve-before-create path (same `resolveStockLine`) вњ… В· (c) `stockLineResolver` L5 size-picker gate (`detectSizeCollision`) вњ… unaffected вЂ” the four are one row each, so L4 returns `matches.length===1` and resolves directly В· (d) `populate.ts:203-206` WRITE path вњ… proven 4-fixed/0-broken В· (e) `searchStockLines:172-179` вњ… (already worked via the substring branch; the token branch now agrees) В· (f) `canonicalName.test.ts` вњ… 21/21 pre-existing green.

**CONSTRAINTS HELD:** AC-1 (no vertical noun вЂ” the resolver names only `business_inventory`) В· AC-3 (probe + all reads tenant-scoped) В· В§6 r8 (ONE tokenizer, ONE equality engine `tokenSetsEqual` вЂ” no fork) В· В§6 r11 (12/12 api-fn, none minted) В· STD-001 (read-only proof first) В· STD-002 (REDв†’GREEN artifacts) В· STD-003 (`[TRACE:*]` extended, nothing commented out) В· STD-011 В· STD-017 В· D-9.

**`[TRACE:RESOLVE]` EXTENDED (the diagnostic that was missing):** the L4 **hit** now logs `key:` (the normalized token set the equality actually compared), and вЂ” the valuable one вЂ” the **MISS** now logs `[TRACE:RESOLVE] L4 MISS вЂ” no name-token match for <tag> key: <key> (tokens: [...])`. A false-UNKNOWN is exactly the #55 class: the tag was RIGHT and the key silently disagreed by one eaten character. That trail is what makes the next one visible instead of a shrug.

**OWNER-PROVEN owed (David, live):** **(1)** Scan each of the four в†’ resolves at L4 to the right row, NOT UNKNOWN вЂ” **Basham's Party Pink Crape Myrtle в†’ `91be4388-6932-4aba-b859-c4f95ed76dfd`** (DISC-1009) В· **Evey's Pride Mimosa в†’ `970aa781-49ae-4184-afe7-15268d7a3138`** (DISC-1070) В· **Summer's Tower Redbud в†’ `076a20a1-f1b9-40bd-9de8-7580468d586f`** (DISC-1099) В· **Hearts A'fire Redbud в†’ `518dd451-faf7-4214-837e-a46afd3785d8`** (DISC-1091). **(2)** `[TRACE:RESOLVE] L4 name-token` shows a **`key:`** field (e.g. `key: bashams crape myrtle party pink`). **(3)** `'Sierra' Mexican Red Oak` UNREGRESSED вЂ” both sizes resolve, still share `variant_group=sierra-mexican-red-oak`, size-picker still fires. **(4)** The other 112 rows scan exactly as before. `[TRACE:*]` stays ON until owner-proven. вљ пёЏ **DEPLOYED bar FIRST (В§9 вЂ” committed в‰  live):** the signal ONLY this build emits is the **`key:` field on `[TRACE:RESOLVE]`**; the OLD line has no `key:`. **If a variety still scans UNKNOWN or `key:` is absent, you are testing OLD code вЂ” hard-refresh, confirm the Vercel deploy is READY and the bundle hash changed, and do NOT declare pass/fail** (the #128/#129 stale-cached-bundle scar).

**FLAGGED FOR DAVID (named, NOT built):** **(a)** **`variant_group` backfill for the four is a SEPARATE D-45 concern вЂ” deliberately not built, no SQL written this pass.** All four are `variant_group` NULL, but the scan **RESOLVES without it** (one row per variety в†’ `matches.length===1` в†’ resolved via name); the group only matters when a 2nd size is added to one of them (then `detectSizeCollision` needs it). Not a blocker for the owner-prove. **(b)** **`personName.ts` vs `nameTokenSet` вЂ” both now elide apostrophes; surfaced under В§6 r10, NOT merged.** This is NOT two representations of one fact (STD-011): "same PERSON?" and "same PLANT?" are two different facts with different rules вЂ” `personName` drops no botanical connectors (`var`/`ssp`/`subsp`/`cv`) and its 1-char filter means "a middle initial is not identity," not "kill the hybrid `x`". D-47 already recorded this reasoning; they legitimately share only the ONE equality engine `tokenSetsEqual`, which both already reuse. **(c)** The live catalog is **116 rows, not the 111** the prompt cited вЂ” worth reconciling wherever 111 is written down. **(d)** `nameTokenSet` also turns an HTML numeric entity (`&#039;`) into a stray numeric token via the existing `&`в†’space rule вЂ” **NOT present in live data** (probed all 116 names: only U+0027, zero entities), so deliberately not handled; it would be speculative work (В§6 r10 over-engineering trap).

<!-- Archived 2026-07-16 from CLAUDE.md В§3 HANDOFF вЂ” the FIRST application of the N=3 retention rule (OP-13; ledger #134). Entries dated 2026-07-16 (D-48) through 2026-07-13 (D-39) вЂ” 12 entries, moved VERBATIM, nothing summarized or condensed. CLAUDE.md В§3 retains the newest three: the 2026-07-16 retention entry, D-49 (count-fills-the-stub), and #55 (apostrophe elide). Rule + statement of force: docs/operating-doctrine/end-of-session-protocol.md в†’ GATE вЂ” CLAUDE.md В§3 HANDOFF RETENTION. -->

### 2026-07-16 вЂ” THUNDER SERVICE PRICE OVERRIDE **IS A DISCOUNT** (D-48 proposed): the fix for the QBO `6070` invoice rejection вЂ” the D-43 invariant was SPECCED but never ENFORCED at compute time, so an override wrote a line that contradicted itself; QBO was the FIRST check anywhere that multiplied rate Г— qty; BUILDER-COMPLETE, owner-proof owed; ZERO migration, ZERO api-fn (12/12)

**Type:** App code вЂ” 1 shared spine (`packages/shared/src/business-logic/tierPricing.ts` + its `.test.ts`) + 6 cultivar (`api/orders/submit.ts` В· `api/qbo/invoice/cultivar.ts` В· `src/pages/CartReview.tsx` В· `src/pages/Confirmation.tsx` В· `src/pages/OrderDetail.tsx` В· `src/components/checkout/OrderTotals.tsx` В· `src/hooks/useSubmitOrder.ts`). **NO migration** (`override_reason` already existed on BOTH `order_service_selections` and `order_items` since `20260708_service_override_leakage.sql:27,36`, applied вЂ” submit already WROTE it; it was simply never REQUIRED). **ZERO new `api/` file** (12/12). `[TRACE:PRICE]`/`[TRACE:LEAKAGE]`/`[TRACE:QBO]` ON (every line now logs `invariantOk`). `npm run verify` **exit 0 zero NET-NEW** (tsc 5 / eslint 248 / knip 10В·14В·15 == baseline); `build:cultivar` clean 2350 modules; esbuild OK both endpoints; **138/138 tier-pricing tests** (was 84; +54, **RED-first per STD-002**). Decision **D-48 (proposed)** (`docs/decisions/2026-07-16-service-price-override-is-a-discount-D48.md`). Ledger row #131.

**THE SCAR (proven live, CLV-20260716-1156 вЂ” do NOT re-derive):** `[TRACE:PRICE]` Placement Service = `retailUnit 225 В· qty 7 В· retailTotal 1575 В· discountPct 0 В· discountAmt 0 В· netTotal 1000`. **`1575 в€’ 0 в‰  1000` вЂ” the line CONTRADICTED ITSELF.** The override wrote `netTotal` and left `discountAmt` at zero, so the record said in one breath both "7 Г— $225 with nothing off" and "this costs $1000." **QBO was the FIRST thing downstream that multiplied**: it got `UnitPrice 225 Г— Qty 7 = 1575`, saw `Amount 1000`, and REJECTED the invoice вЂ” `6070 "Amount is not equal to UnitPrice * Qty. Supplied value:1,000"` (reproduced on 2 pushes, 16:57 + 17:19; **the invoice does not exist in QuickBooks**).

**WHY NOTHING CAUGHT IT (the lesson):** every TRACE surface rendered $1000 and reconciled cleanly (Subtotal $2020 В· Tax 7.6% $153.52 В· Total $2173.52 вЂ” all arithmetically correct). The *totals* were right. **No TRACE surface checks that a LINE is internally consistent** вЂ” every check we had looked at *aggregates ACROSS* lines, and a sum of nets is right regardless of whether each line's own arithmetic holds. The defect lived in a relationship *within* one line. **ROOT: D-43 SPECCED the invariant (`retail_total в€’ discount_amt === net_total`) and verified it in a MIGRATION вЂ” which passed ONLY because no override existed yet.** Nothing enforced it where lines are BORN. **An invariant that is only verified in a migration is not enforced.**

**BUILT (David's ruling вЂ” an override IS a discount; SUPERSEDES D-39's "leakage в‰  discount" service branch):**
- **(1) OVERRIDE-AS-DISCOUNT** вЂ” the retail baseline (`retailUnit`/`qty`/`retailTotal`) is **PRESERVED** (what makes the giveaway visible AND what lets QBO multiply and agree); the concession lands in `discountAmt 575` / `discountPct 36.51` (**convention stated:** `round2(discountAmt / retailTotal Г— 100)`). The tier still NEVER touches a service.
- **(2) THE INVARIANT IS ENFORCED AT COMPUTE TIME AND *THROWS*** вЂ” asserted on every line inside `computeOrderPricing` with a half-cent tolerance (`round2` of a float difference leaves dust: `0.3 в€’ round2(0.3в€’0.1) в‰  0.1`). Unfireable by construction в†’ it is the guard against the NEXT branch that forgets. Throwing is right: a self-contradictory money line is a programming error, and refusing an order beats billing an incoherent one.
- **(3) UPWARD override = a NEGATIVE discount (surcharge) вЂ” ALLOWED** (decided + stated). Refusing would **REGRESS an ability the owner has today** (the code caps nothing вЂ” an upward override already charges more) and it's legitimate (rocky site в†’ extra labour). Stays consistent (`1575 в€’ (в€’425) = 2000`), pushes a POSITIVE "price adjusted" line, no branch. `discountPct` honest-negative вЂ” a real number, not nonsense; **never rendered as "% off"** (neutral "price adjusted" wording reads correctly both ways; CartReview's old `Math.max(0, вЂ¦)` rendered *nothing* for a surcharge вЂ” now fixed).
- **(4) вљ пёЏ `discountTotal` RESTRICTED TO GOODS-ONLY + NEW `serviceAdjustmentTotal` вЂ” the structural trap the spec did NOT anticipate.** `discountTotal`'s contract already SAID "ОЈ discountAmt (goods only)" but it summed **every** line and merely got away with it *because services were hardcoded to `discountAmt: 0`*. The moment a service override became a discount, the $575 would have leaked into the roll-up Review/Confirmation render as **"<tier> вЂ” N% off"** against `goodsRetailSubtotal` в†’ "Contractor tier 1 вЂ” 10% off в€’$714.60" on a customer whose tier discount was $139.60. **Two proven surfaces would have started lying, and the totals would still have summed correctly.** STD-011: a tier discount and an owner concession are two DIFFERENT facts (different authority, label, audit trail) вЂ” *one representation of two facts* is the same disease inverted.
- **(5) REASON REQUIRED (STD-013, reusing D-40's exemption shape вЂ” no second mechanism):** UI (`saveEditor` blocks Apply; labelled `Reason *` + a surfaced error, not a silent no-op вЂ” В§6 r15/M2) **and** server (`applyOverride` REFUSES, logs, charges the **BASELINE** вЂ” refusal charges MORE, the money-safe direction) **and** spine (`computeOrderPricing` enforces it independently, so the gates agree *by construction*). **NO MIGRATION** вЂ” the column existed and was written; it was never *required*. David applied a $575 giveaway with the field **blank** and it went through.
- **(6) THE QBO LINE** вЂ” an overridden service pushes at its **RETAIL baseline** (`225 Г— 7 = 1575`, internally consistent в†’ accepted) + the concession on the **SAME** negative-Amount mechanism as the tier discount, described `"Placement Service вЂ” price adjusted (reason: loyal contractor)"`. **STD-011:** extracted ONE `discountLine()` helper used by BOTH the goods tier discount (D-43) and the service override вЂ” no forked second discount path. **GATED** on an override applying в†’ a normal order pushes **byte-identical** (zero regression; `unit_price_at_time Г— quantity === subtotal` holds for every non-overridden row since flat services store qty 1). NEW **reconcile check**: the assembled lines must SUM to the order total or the push is REFUSED вЂ” so a discount line can never double-count against an already-netted line, and TRACE never sends an amount it didn't charge.
- **(7) CONFIRMATION REPORTED THE FAILURE HONESTLY (D-9)** вЂ” the push hard-failed (400) and the page rendered *"вЏ± Invoice will sync to QuickBooks shortly вЂ” Connect QuickBooks from the owner dashboard."* Wrong twice: QBO **was** connected (it minted customer 84 seconds earlier), and nothing would **ever** sync. **The cause was structural: the type was `'success' | 'pending'` вЂ” there was NO failed state to render**, so every failure fell into a bucket that promised a sync. Now THREE honest states (`QbSyncStatus`): `success` В· `not_connected` (**503 only** вЂ” the connect prompt is right *here and only here*) В· `failed` (400/409/500/throw вЂ” say so, name the reason, give the action). Scoped by `ownerView`: owner sees the real error + next step; the customer sees a true neutral state ("Order confirmed вЂ” invoice to follow") with **no internals and no owner instruction** вЂ” which the old copy was handing to customers on the public QR path.
